t_ch: 小灰，请你在心底呼唤「万径之门」，感受空间的摇曳，聆听遥远的彼方在你耳边的呢喃……
t_en: Gray Hair, please call out the "Gate of Infinity" from the bottom of your heart. Feel the wavering of space and listen to the distant murmurs in your ears...

t_ch: 然后，把你感受到的这股力量，像是撒网一样丢出来！
t_en: Then, cast the power that you sense out like it's a net!
Hya!!!

t_ch: …小灰，吓*我们*一跳。但门径好像没打开诶……
t_en: ...Gray Hair, you scared us! And it doesn't seem that the gate is opened...

t_ch: 呼——轰炸空间！
t_en: Ha! Space bomb!

t_ch: 不对不对！小灰用力过猛啦，听上去好像要把空间都给消灭掉啦！
t_en: No, no! That's far too violent, Gray Hair! It sounds like you want to completely annihilate space itself!

t_ch: 感受不到，不如界域定锚。
t_en: I cannot feel it. Shall I use a Space Anchor?

t_ch: 那是什么…难道是*我们*不知道的雅努斯神迹吗？
t_en: What is that? Is it some kind of miracle of Janus that we do not know about?

t_ch: 看来，学习施展「百界门」对于小灰来说还是过于勉强了。不过不要难过，小灰很厉害！不需要「百界门」，也一定能成为翁法罗斯的大英雄！
t_en: It seems that learning to use the "Century Gate" is a bit too difficult for you now, Gray Hair. But don't be disheartened. You are already amazing! We know that you will become a great hero of Amphoreus even without the power of the "Century Gate"!

t_ch: 你们的心灵感应好方便。
t_en: Your telepathy must be very convenient.

t_ch: 啊，是说*我们*和缇安、缇宁之间的心灵感应吗？
t_en: Oh, you mean the telepathy shared by Tribbie, Trianne, and Trinnon?

t_ch: 嗯，绝大部分时候都很方便。只要愿意，*我们*三人可以共享感官所触的大部分事物。
t_en: Yes, it is, most of the time. As long as we are willing, we can share most of our senses.

t_ch: 比如说，我和缇宁平时不大爱出门，而缇安很喜欢到处游玩。看到漂亮景色的时候，缇安就会分享给*我们*。
t_en: For instance, Tribbie and Trinnon are the indoorsy type, but Trianne is the opposite. When Trianne sees a beautiful landscape, she shares it with us through telepathy.

t_ch: 再比如，当阿雅需要派人去到很远的、金线没有布及的地方时，就会让他们带上*我们*中的一人。这样，就算传信石版不管用，阿雅也可以及时了解到第一手信息啦。
t_en: Also, when Agy needs to send people to a distant place out of the golden thread's reach, she'll ask one of us to join them. That way, even if teleslates won't work, Agy can reach us in time.

t_ch: 奥赫玛最棒小信使！
t_en: A round of applause for Okhema's greatest messengers!

t_ch: 说得*我们*都不好意思啦。但其实，心灵感应也有不方便的时候哦。
t_en: Stop it, we are blushing. Actually, telepathy doesn't always come in handy.

t_ch: 那什么时候会不方便呢？
t_en: Like when?

t_ch: 在情感波动比较剧烈的时候，*我们*会下意识地共享给彼此。比如说，有一人不小心撞到脑袋，另外两个也会疼得叫出声来。
t_en: When our emotions are too intense, we will subconsciously share them. For example, if one of us accidentally bangs our head, the others will feel the pain.

t_ch: 曾经发生过这样一件好笑的事情：缇宁在图书馆读到一个叫人难过的故事，忍不住掉起眼泪。
t_en: There's a funny story. Trinnon once read a sad story in the library and couldn't help but let out a few sobs.

t_ch: 那时候缇安在云石市集正玩得开心，突然就开始抽噎了。街坊们都被吓到，一个劲儿地往缇安怀里塞好吃的来安慰她呢！
t_en: At that moment, Trianne was out having a wonderful time in Marmoreal Market and she suddenly started sobbing too. Seeing that, everyone was worried and shocked. So they tried to stop her from crying by handing Trianne loads of tasty food.

t_ch: 所以假如有一天，小灰看到*我们*忽然掉眼泪，也不要太吃惊哦。
t_en: So, if one day you see us suddenly start shedding tears out of nowhere, no need to worry.

t_ch: 下次再聊，缇宝明天见！
t_en: Let's talk another time. I'll see you tomorrow, Tribbie!

t_ch: 好呀好呀，明天见可是个特别、特别好的祝福呢。小灰明天见！
t_en: Okay, sure! "See you tomorrow" is such a wonderful blessing! See you tomorrow, Gray Hair!

t_ch: 下次再聊，缇宝明天见！
t_en: Let's talk another time. I'll see you tomorrow, Tribbie!

t_ch: 好呀好呀，明天见可是个特别、特别好的祝福呢。小灰明天见！
t_en: Okay, sure! "See you tomorrow" is such a wonderful blessing! See you tomorrow, Gray Hair!

t_ch: 哼哼哼~缇安今天心情也很好！准备去把奥赫玛再飞个遍！
t_en: Hum-hum-hum! Trianne is in a great mood today as well! Time for a flight over all Okhema again!

t_ch: 圣城…大家都很喜欢这里，远道而来的朋友，希望你也是。
t_en: The holy city... Everyone seems to like this place. I hope that you do too, friend from afar!

t_ch: …人之所以独处，是为了独自享用清静——这么简单的道理，你应当明白吧？
t_en: ...The reason people spend time alone is to enjoy peace and quiet... You must understand such a simple logic, surely?

t_ch: 别这么凶，就聊几句。
t_en: There's no need to be so harsh. Let's have a chat.

t_ch: 也罢。有什么想说的？
t_en: Fine. What do you wish to say?

t_ch: 尼卡多利是个怎样的泰坦？
t_en: What kind of Titan is Nikador?

t_ch: 众所周知——战争之神，恐惧之神，荣耀之神。
t_en: As is known to all... Nikador is the God of War, the God of Fear, and the God of Glory.

t_ch: 当然，也有人将其视为蛮愚与暴戾的泰坦，称其为「疯王」…倒也不无道理，对于羔羊来说，野狼的哈欠都足以使其破胆。
t_en: Of course, there are those that see Nikador as foolish and violent. They call Nikador the "Mad King"... It does make sense. To a flock of sheep, even a yawn from a wolf is enough to send them into hysteria.

t_ch: 现在它已陨，再无战争！
t_en: They are dead now, and there will be no more wars!

t_ch: 现在松懈还太早，仍有纷争的眷属在世间徘徊，黑潮也仍在肆虐。只有当战争的目的一并消弭，真正的安宁才能够降临。
t_en: It is too early to be so complacent. Titankin of Strife still roam this world, and the black tide rages on. Only when the true purpose of war is eradicated can peace descend.

t_ch: 当生存的危机悬于头顶，是求饶苟延，还是拔剑赴战，即使你不是悬锋人，想必也能给出正确的回答。
t_en: When a threat to your survival looms over your head, you have but two options: To beg for mercy and buy time, or to draw your sword and go into battle. I'm sure you do not have to be a Kremnoan to know the right answer.

t_ch: 恐惧与荣耀，不矛盾么？
t_en: Fear and glory... Is that not a contradiction?

t_ch: 悬锋人历来将恐惧视作踏向荣耀的道路。尼卡多利之所以令悬锋人誓死相随，正是因为它能将无边恐惧与无上荣耀锻作一炉。
t_en: To the Kremnoans, fear is the path to glory. The reason that Kremnoans vow to follow Nikador to their death is so that Nikador can forge limitless fear into endless glory.

t_ch: 纷争泰坦尚未失神的辉煌年代，只有在其威压之下仍能稳持长枪、浴血而立的王储，才有资格继任悬锋之王。
t_en: In the glorious era before the Strife Titan fell, only the crown prince who could hold their spear firm under the divine pressure and stand tall under the rain of blood was worthy to become the King of Kremnos.

t_ch: 我能理解奥赫玛人对尼卡多利的嫌怨，但我也希望你明白，尼卡多利给予翁法罗斯的不止痛苦与恐惧。
t_en: I can understand the Okhemans' grudge against Nikador. However, I do hope that you will understand that Nikador has gifted more than just suffering and fear to Amphoreus.

t_ch: 在天谴之矛陷入疯溃前，它也曾是翁法罗斯的守护者。纷争的力量，亦可平息纷争。
t_en: Before the Lance of Fury fell into madness, they served as the guardian of Amphoreus. The power of Strife is also the queller of strife.

t_ch: 想听听悬锋孤军的来历。
t_en: I want to hear about the origins of the Kremnoan Detachment.

t_ch: 呵，这可是个颇长的故事…我就只讲些片段吧。
t_en: Hah, that is a long story... Let me give you the abridged version.

t_ch: 不知你是否听闻，我曾与自己的故乡有些过节。出于某些原因，我选择了离开悬锋城，在翁法罗斯各地流亡，悬锋孤军则是愿追随我同行的一众乡友。
t_en: I'm not sure if you have heard this before, but I had a few issues with my hometown. I elected to leave Castrum Kremnos for various reasons and wander across Amphoreus. The Kremnoan Detachment was a group of my fellow countrymen who were willing to follow me.

t_ch: 虽是流亡，但他们的锐气比起悬锋军将有过之而无不及。拉冬、哀地里亚、厄涅俄努斯，皆有我等威名传颂。
t_en: Though they were exiles, their drive exceeded even that of the soldiers of Kremnos. Word of our might spread across Ladon, Aidonia, and Aenionus.

t_ch: 后来机缘巧合下，我们与奥赫玛结为联盟，为「金织」主持的逐火之旅效力。期间，悬锋孤军战功赫赫，并在征途中杀回王城，令先王败而授首。
t_en: Later, we happened to ally ourselves with Okhema and offered our services to the Flame-Chase Journey led by the Goldweaver. During that time, this lone army of Kremnos achieved great military success. Along the expedition, we fought back into their home city and defeated the previous king.

t_ch: 好一个冷血王道故事！
t_en: What a chilling story of royal leadership!

t_ch: 或许此番讲述还是太过云淡风轻。在那段征途中，我等经历的磨难与牺牲，绝比言辞要沉重千万倍。
t_en: Perhaps this narration is an understatement. The suffering and sacrifices that we experienced during that expedition were heavier than any words could convey.

t_ch: 你们会想家么？
t_en: Do you folks miss home?

t_ch: 我自幼离乡，虽流淌着一脉相承的悬锋之血，但对那座王都没有那么眷恋。
t_en: I left my hometown at a young age. Though I carry the blood of Castrum Kremnos, I bear no affection for that kingdom.

t_ch: 至于我的同胞们…悬锋人思乡的方式，就是在战场上恣意拼杀。当他们在战场上染血高呼着「宁战死，毋荣归」的时候，我就知道…奥赫玛，无法成为他们真正的家。
t_en: As for my kin... the Kremnoans show how much they miss their home by fighting without restraint. When I see them on the battlefield, blood-stained and raising a cry of "valorous death before glorious return," I knew... that Okhema could never be their true home.

t_ch: 就讲这么多吧。如果你有兴趣，也可去找那些真言狮口再问些悬锋孤军的事迹。不过切勿轻信——那群家伙最爱东拼西凑、搬弄是非。
t_en: That is all I will say for now. If you are interested, you can go to those Verax Leos and ask about the achievements of the Kremnoan Detachment. But do not trust their every word... They love to piece together stories to stir the pot.

t_ch: 为什么不是你去尼卡多利的试炼？
t_en: Why are you not taking the trial of Nikador?

t_ch: …白厄比我更合适。
t_en: ...Phainon is better suited for that than I am.

t_ch: 我看你们打得不相上下呀。
t_en: You two seem evenly matched.

t_ch: 决定谁去承载纷争火种的不只有武力的高低。白厄他…是无缺的「救世主」，由他接纳尼卡多利的神性再合适不过。
t_en: It is not merely martial strength that decides who will bear the Coreflame of Strife. Phainon... he is the flawless "Deliverer," and there is no greater candidate to inherit the divinity of Nikador.

t_ch: 只要他能克服内心的恐惧，荣耀自会成为他的冠冕。
t_en: As long as he can overcome the fear in his heart, glory shall become his crown.

t_ch: 你害怕自己通不过试炼？
t_en: Are you scared that you would not pass the trial?

t_ch: 哼，与尼卡多利的决战都不足为惧，区区试炼有何可怕？但阻止我手握火种的并非泰坦的试炼…而是悬锋城的试炼。
t_en: Hmph. If a battle to the death with Nikador could not incite fear in my heart, what could scare me about a mere trial? The thing that stops me from grasping the Coreflame is not the trial of the Titan... but the trial of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 容我打断你的追问，我拒绝接收火种自有我的道理。我不会因你而动摇自己的决定，无需再费口舌。
t_en: Allow me to interrupt your interrogation here. I have my own reasons for not accepting the Coreflame. I will not allow my decision to be shaken by your intentions. You may save your breath.

t_ch: 好像没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 言尽于此。再会。
t_en: Farewell.

t_ch: 抱歉，我这就走。
t_en: Sorry. I'll be gone.

t_ch: 再会。
t_en: Farewell.

t_ch: 想请你帮我翻译一块石板。
t_en: I need your help to translate a slate.

t_ch: 给我看看。
t_en: Let me see.

t_ch: 你怎么不说话？
t_en: Say something!

t_ch: 你是从哪儿找到这块石板的？悬锋城么？
t_en: Where did you find this slate? Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 石板上都说了些什么？
t_en: What does the slate say?

t_ch: 一段往事罢了，你自己看吧。说起来，你不是得到欧洛尼斯的赐福了么？
t_en: Just some stories of the past, take a look for yourself. Come to think of it, did you not receive the benediction of Oronyx?

t_ch: 要是感兴趣，不如去悬锋城转转，说不定能在记忆的尘堆里翻出些有趣的旧史呢。
t_en: If you're interested, why not take a look around Castrum Kremnos for yourself? Maybe you will be able to dig up some interesting stories from among the dust of memories.

t_ch: 你还想追问些什么，但万敌别过脸去，对这个话题缄口不言。
t_en: Though you have more questions you want to ask, Mydei turns away and remains silent on the topic.

t_ch: 请小心些，{NICKNAME}阁下，别靠太近…抱歉，这只是为了安全，并非对您有什么意见。
t_en: Please be careful, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. Don't get too close... Sorry, this is for your safety. I do not have any objection to your presence.

t_ch: 阁下找我，是有什么话想说吗？
t_en: Is there something you wish to discuss with me, {F#miss}{M#mister}?

t_ch: 遐蝶，来聊会儿天吧。
t_en: Castorice, let's chat.

t_ch: 我很乐意…但我平时很少与人打交道，有些不善交流…希望阁下不要介意。
t_en: I would love to... although I very rarely converse with others. I am not gifted with communication... Please do not be offended, {F#miss}{M#mister}.

t_ch: 平时喜欢做些什么？
t_en: What do you like to do normally?

t_ch: 看书…扫墓…看花…还有做些小手工。
t_en: Read books... visit graves... admiring flowers... and make ornaments.

t_ch: 好小众的爱好。
t_en: Very niche hobbies.

t_ch: 嗯，因为诅咒，我不便与他人同行，只能寻些独处的喜好。
t_en: Indeed. It is difficult for me to work alongside others due to my curse. I have no choice but to find hobbies I can enjoy alone.

t_ch: 看看手工。
t_en: Let me see what you made.

t_ch: 啊…我身上这些蝴蝶与花的饰品就是……
t_en: Ah... I made all of these butterfly and flower accessories on my dress...

t_ch: 可爱捏。
t_en: So cute!

t_ch: 唔，可爱…谢谢。
t_en: Oh, cute... Thank you.

t_ch: 奥赫玛所有的藏书我都已经读过，其中尤其偏爱历史与文学。我自己最喜欢的一则故事讲的是公主变成巨龙……
t_en: I have read all of the books in the Okhema archives. I prefer books on history and culture. My favorite story is about a princess that becomes a dragon...

t_ch: 请别嘲笑我，传说是跨越万千岁月而诞生的珍贵结晶。比如最广为人知的翁法罗斯泰坦神话，如果你有机会了解，一定会被它的恢弘与精妙所震撼的。
t_en: Please do not laugh at me. A legend is a precious crystal that is born over the course of millennia. Take the famous Titan mythology of Amphoreus. If you have time to learn about it, you will be shocked by its vastness and exquisite detail.

t_ch: 至于扫墓…奥赫玛的墓园里，有许多长眠者，都是由我来「入殓」的。我会定期前去清扫他们的坟墓——不知为何，我总担忧他们未能安眠。
t_en: As for visiting graves... Many of the deceased around the graveyards of Okhema were personally embalmed by me. I periodically go to their tombs to dust... I'm not sure why but I'm always concerned that somehow they cannot rest in peace.

t_ch: 手工则是因为我想多保留一些美丽的事物。蝴蝶、干花，我会把它们缝到特制的绸布上，让它们看上去好像从未失去生命。
t_en: Making ornaments is how I preserve things of beauty, such as butterflies and dried flowers... I sew them onto special silk fabric so they look like they never lost their lives.

t_ch: 为了尽可能不损伤它们，我还特地去找阿格莱雅大人学了些针线手艺。若阁下不嫌弃，我之后也可以送你些做的东西。
t_en: I learned sewing techniques from lady Aglaea to ensure that I do not damage them in the process. I could gift you some in the future if you'd like to have some.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的居民都挺怕你的。
t_en: The people of Okhema seem to fear you.

t_ch: 这也无可厚非，因为我是圣城的「入殓师」，曾经…更是哀地里亚的督战圣女。在大家眼中，我就是行走的死亡使者。
t_en: They cannot be blamed for this. I am the "mortician" in the holy city. I was also once... the holy maiden who oversaw warfare in Aidonia. In their eyes, I am no more than a walking envoy of death.

t_ch: 想听听你的故事。
t_en: I would like to hear your story.

t_ch: 我并无来到哀地里亚之前的记忆；据他们说，我被哀地里亚收留以前一直在荒原上彷徨游荡。
t_en: I have no memories of the time before I came to Aidonia. From what I'm told, I was merely a drifter who wandered the wastelands until Aidonia took me in.

t_ch: 想听听哀地里亚的故事。
t_en: I'd like to hear stories about Aidonia.

t_ch: 哀地里亚是一座信仰塞纳托斯的城邦，居民们视死如归——与悬锋人的渴望战死不同…他们将死亡作为生的幸福归宿。
t_en: Aidonia is a city that worships Thanatos. Its residents regard death as their destined end... Unlike the Kremnoans longing to die in battle, the Aidonians see death as the blessed final destination for life.

t_ch: 不过这并不意味着哀地里亚人不珍惜生命。相反，他们认为只有在世间行走磨砺足够长久，才能有坚韧的、足以渡过冥河面见塞纳托斯的灵魂。
t_en: However, that does not mean that Aidonians do not treasure life. On the contrary, they believe that it is only by living a long life and tempering oneself will one gain a soul tenacious enough to cross the River of Souls and meet Thanatos.

t_ch: 哀地里亚人在荒原中找到我，坚信我身负死亡泰坦赐福，我的双手能让他们避开冥河、直接面见伟大的塞纳托斯。
t_en: The Aidonian found me in the wilds. They strongly believed that I had received the blessing of the Death Titan, that my hands could spare them from the River of Souls and send them straight to the great Thanatos.

t_ch: 因此我被奉为「督战圣女」——哀地里亚人在战场上牺牲的将士，将由我赋以独属于死者的抚慰与尊严。
t_en: So, I was revered as the "Maiden of War"... Only I could grant the deceased Aidonian warriors the comfort and respect that they deserve.

t_ch: 给我讲些泰坦的故事吧！
t_en: Tell me about the Titans!

t_ch: 想不到阁下也对翁法罗斯的历史神话故事感兴趣呀！正巧，漫长岁月里我攒了不少有趣的故事，不知阁下想听什么样的？
t_en: I never would have thought that you were also interested in the mythological tales of Amphoreus, {F#miss}{M#mister}! As luck would have it, I have collected many interesting stories over my long years. What kind of tale would you like to hear?

t_ch: 我要听正史！
t_en: I want to hear a formal historical account!

t_ch: 或许要让阁下失望了，翁法罗斯的大部分历史都是由吟游诗人们自遥远的过去带来，再由他人录为文字，如此口口相传，总有失真之处。
t_en: I fear you may be disappointed, {F#miss}{M#mister}. The majority of Amphoreus' history comes from bards recounting stories of ancient times. These stories are then recorded into words by third parties. Distortions of the truth are inevitable.

t_ch: 如今，浴场精灵们所讲述的翁法罗斯创世神话，或许是目前最符合阁下想要的「正史」了。
t_en: Perhaps the story that best fits the description of "a formal historical account" is the myth of Amphoreus' creation that the bath sprites have been telling.

t_ch: 不过如果阁下不介意，我还有其它一些有趣的故事可以与你分享。
t_en: However, I have other interesting stories that I could share with you. That is, if you do not mind.

t_ch: 我要听野史！
t_en: I want to hear historical rumors and gossip!

t_ch: 如果阁下不介意，我确实有些有趣的故事可以与你分享。
t_en: I do have a number of interesting stories that I could share with you. That is, if you do not mind.

t_ch: 譬如…扎格列斯的故事。
t_en: For instance... The story of Zagreus.

t_ch: 相传扎格列斯喜欢对其他诸神恶作剧，曾偷走塔兰顿的天平和法吉娜的酒杯。诸神为了遏制它的恶行，就在它身上绑了一颗星星，用于暴露扎格列斯的去向。
t_en: Legend has it that Zagreus used to enjoy playing pranks on the other gods, stealing Talanton's scales and Phagousa's wine glass. To put an end to their misdoings, the gods fastened a star to Zagreus' body to reveal their whereabouts at all times.

t_ch: 又比如，法吉娜与吉奥里亚两尊泰坦相看两厌，翁法罗斯的下雨天其实是法吉娜在灌醉艾格勒后对吉奥里亚发出的挑衅。
t_en: Another story that I have heard is that the Titans Phagousa and Georios cannot stand each other. Legend states that when it rains in Amphoreus, this is actually Phagousa taunting Georios after having gotten Aquila drunk.

t_ch: 而吉奥里亚与瑟希斯达成盟约，让植物们替自己挡住法吉娜不息的波涛声和雨声。
t_en: So, Georios formed an alliance with Cerces to have plants shelter the land and block the sound of Phagousa's endless waves and rain.

t_ch: 还有更吊诡的传说：据说尼卡多利曾经对塔兰顿……
t_en: There are even more bizarre legends than these: It is said that Nikador did something to Talanton, which is...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，一不留神我居然这么多话，耽误了阁下好多时间。我很少有机会能和朋友如此畅聊…真的很谢谢你。
t_en: Ah, my apologies. I got caught up in my stories and ended up rambling. I'm so sorry to have wasted your time. I rarely get an opportunity to speak so freely with a friend... I am truly grateful.

t_ch: 没什么话要说啦，再见！
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about. Goodbye!

t_ch: 好的，再见，下次有机会再一起聊聊天吧。
t_en: Okay, farewell. Let's talk again when we get a chance.

t_ch: 没什么话要说啦，再见！
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about. Goodbye!

t_ch: 好的，再见，下次有机会再一起聊聊天吧。
t_en: Okay, farewell. Let's talk again when we get a chance.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯在上，请指引我家人的魂灵……
t_en: Thanatos above, please, guide the souls of my family...

t_ch: 哦，抱歉。刚才在祈祷，没注意到你。你也是新来的吗？你好，我叫布鲁图斯，刚到这儿没多久。
t_en: Oh, my apologies. I was praying and did not notice you. Are you new here, too? Hello, my name is Brutus. I arrived not long ago.

t_ch: 你在祈祷什么？
t_en: What were you praying for?

t_ch: 我在为我的家人祈祷，愿他们能享有永恒的安宁。
t_en: I was saying a prayer for my family. I wish they may have eternal peace.

t_ch: 你从哪里过来的？
t_en: Where are you from?

t_ch: 我的故乡啊…那是离奥赫玛最近的度假胜地，气候极为舒适…我家后院有座超级大的花园，我妻子在里面种满了她最爱的薄暮玫瑰。
t_en: My home... It is the closest scenic holiday spot to Okhema. The climate there is wonderful... My home had a gigantic garden in the rear courtyard. My wife filled it with her favorite dusk roses.

t_ch: …抱歉，我的措辞有误，那里曾是离奥赫玛最近的度假胜地，嗯…*曾是*。
t_en: ...Sorry, I should rephrase that. It was the closest scenic holiday spot to Okhema... Was.

t_ch: …黑潮来的时候，我正在给花园浇水。谁知道那会是最后一次照看那些薄暮玫瑰呢。
t_en: ...When the black tide came, I was watering the garden. Who knew that would be the last time I tended to those dusk roses...

t_ch: 我妻子特别喜欢那些玫瑰。每天早上她都会摘一朵别在头发上。现在想想，真该多种些的。
t_en: My wife loved those roses... Every morning she would pick one and pin it to her hair. Thinking about it now, I should really have planted some more.

t_ch: 本来想投奔这儿的老朋友，结果一到就听说他前段时间...唉，算了。看来这世道，谁也说不准明天会怎样。
t_en: I came here to seek shelter with an old friend but, when I arrived, I heard that he just recently... Ah, forget it. I guess no one in this world can ever really know what tomorrow will bring.

t_ch: 至少我还活着，对吧？希望雅努斯能给我指条明路。虽然有时候我也在想，活着到底是幸运还是不幸…如果我是幸运儿，那为什么只有我一个人侥幸存活呢？
t_en: At least I'm still alive, right? I hope that Janus will guide me through this misery. Although, sometimes I wonder if I'm actually fortunate to be alive or not... Is it really fortunate to be the sole survivor?

t_ch: 节哀顺变。
t_en: I'm sorry for your loss.

t_ch: 谢谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 你是雅努斯的信徒？
t_en: You're a disciple of Janus?

t_ch: 不，在见过黑潮之后，我已经是塞纳托斯的信徒了，我愿用我的余生向塞纳托斯祈祷，只求它能带给我家人永恒的沉眠。
t_en: No. Ever after I saw the black tide, I have become a disciple of Thanatos. I will spend the rest of my life praying to Thanatos for the eternal peace of my family.

t_ch: 你要是跟我一样…不信点什么的话，怕是连床都起不来。
t_en: If you were me... You'd need something to believe in, or you wouldn't even be able to get out of bed in the morning.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯在上…它应该不会让我家人在黑暗里迷路吧？他们应该能找到安静的地方歇着。我儿子小时候最怕黑了，希望他在那边不会害怕。
t_en: By Thanatos above... They would not leave my family lost in the darkness, would they? My family should be able to find a place of peace and quiet to rest. My little son was afraid of the dark. I hope that he is not scared over there.

t_ch: 你知道吗，有时候我会和塞纳托斯说话——都是我单方面提问。我总是一边祈祷，一边在心里问，我家人在那边过得好吗？有没有吃的？冷不冷？可惜，它从不回答。
t_en: You know, sometimes I talk to Thanatos — I ask a lot of questions. When I pray, I always wonder if my family is doing well over there, and if they have enough food and clothes to keep themselves warm. Unfortunately, I never got the answers.

t_ch: 哼，我也有想过…我好吃好喝地供奉着它，肯定不是我不够心诚，想来想去……
t_en: Hmph. I have also considered... I have offered Thanatos fine food and drink. There's no way that I have not been loyal enough. I keep thinking and...

t_ch: 一定是那个马尔库斯，每次我做祷告，他就在那儿嚷嚷，肯定是他惊扰到塞纳托斯了。
t_en: It must be that Marcus. Every time I go to pray, he makes noises. He must have disturbed Thanatos...

t_ch: 他怎么你了？
t_en: What did he do to you?

t_ch: …详细说说？
t_en: ...Do you want to tell me more about it?

t_ch: 喏，就隔壁那个疯子，我喊他「撒谎精」——天天讲他打仗的事。今天说自己杀了十个怪物，明天又说自己躲在树后面看别人打仗，前后矛盾，嘴里就没一句实话。
t_en: Ugh, he's the madman that lives next door. I call him the "braggart"... He is constantly telling stories of battles he's been in, how he killed ten monsters by himself, or how he hid behind a tree and watched others fight them. His stories are self-contradiction itself. You can never trust a single word he says.

t_ch: 最可气的是…他竟然说被卷入黑潮的人都不得安宁！说什么他看到他们队长的脸，总是出现在雾里，什么在黑潮来临的时候，听到同伴喊他进去的声音……
t_en: The worst thing of all is that he claims those who are devoured by the black tide will never be at peace! He says that he sees their captain's face in the mist and hears his comrades calling him to join them in the black tide...

t_ch: 塞纳托斯在上，它平等地眷顾每一个逝者，它的神力至高无上…那个马尔库斯，他是怎么敢的？！
t_en: The Great Thanatos above! Thanatos has equal mercy for the deceased and supreme powers above all... That Marcus, how dare he utter such nonsense!?

t_ch: 抱歉，我有点太激动了，希望没吓到你。
t_en: Sorry, I let myself get carried away. I hope I did not scare you.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯在上，请指引我家人的魂灵…哦，是你啊。有事吗？
t_en: By Thanatos above, please guide the souls of my family... Oh, it's you. What's up?

t_ch: 能和我聊聊你的事吗？
t_en: Can you tell me about yourself?

t_ch: 我的事？没什么好说的，就是个普通人罢了。不过既然你好奇…那就大大方方地问吧。
t_en: About myself? There's not much to say. I'm just a normal Mountain Dweller. Feel free to ask if you are curious.

t_ch: 你到底经历了什么？
t_en: What happened to you?

t_ch: …在黑潮来的那天，我刚把晚饭端上桌。孩子们还在吵着要吃点心。谁知道那会是最后一顿饭。现在想想，真该让他们多吃点的。
t_en: ...On the day the black tide came, I had just put dinner on the table. The children were clamoring that they wanted to eat snacks instead. Who knew that would be the last meal we ever had together? Thinking about it now, I should have made sure they ate more.

t_ch: 你知道吗，我女儿那天还在闹脾气，说爸爸做的饭不好吃。我当时还训了她几句。要是能重来，我一定让她想吃什么就吃什么。
t_en: You know what? That day, my daughter was throwing a tantrum and saying that the food I cooked was horrible. I told her off. If I could go back, I would let her have whatever she wanted.

t_ch: 每天睡前我都在想，是不是该多给他们一块糖。现在想想，真该给的……
t_en: Every day before I sleep, I think to myself... Should I have let them have some more candy? I really should have done it...

t_ch: 最近我总是做梦，梦见他们还在身边。醒来的时候，那种失落感…哎，只有向塞纳托斯祈祷，我的心才能平静下来……
t_en: I keep dreaming lately that they are still here with me. That feeling of loss I experience when I wake up... Ah, only praying to Thanatos can calm my heart...

t_ch: 你好像很讨厌马尔库斯？
t_en: You seem to really hate Marcus?

t_ch: 马尔库斯啊，那个「撒谎精」，他总说自己是个老兵，在对抗黑潮的时候还救过人，可他讲的事情前后矛盾，我觉得他好像在编故事，以此作为某种谈资。
t_en: Marcus, you mean the "braggart"? He's always talking about how he's a veteran. He says that he saved people when he fought off the black tide. But his stories are so full of contradictions! I can't help but feel like he's making them all up just to make his stories sound cool.

t_ch: 不过…就在几天前，我看到他一个人对着墙说话，听到他在小声说「对不起」，虽然不知道是在对谁说，但我突然觉得他可能比我还可怜。
t_en: Although... A few days ago, I saw him alone speaking to a wall. I heard him quietly say: "I'm sorry." I don't know who he was speaking to, but I suddenly felt like he had suffered even more than I have.

t_ch: 活着的人都有自己的苦衷，但我就是不喜欢他这样编排逝者们的往事，这是否有点过于不尊重他们了？所以呀，我对马尔库斯没什么好脸色。
t_en: Life is tough for everybody already. I just don't like how he fabricates the stories of those that passed away. It is sheer disrespect. That's why I have no good words to say about that Marcus.

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 还行吧，我在这儿住不习惯，总觉得少了点什么，可能是因为没有家人在身边吧。
t_en: It's fine. I'm not used to living here yet, I always feel like something is missing. I imagine it's because my family aren't by my side.

t_ch: 在我老家有句话：「塞纳托斯要带走的人，任何神都强留不得。」我时常会拿这句话安慰自己，往事已矣，生者还要好好活下去。
t_en: There is a saying where I come from: "There is no god powerful enough to hold onto those that Thanatos must take away." I often use this phrase to comfort myself. The past is in the past, and the living must make the most of their lives.

t_ch: 哎，不说这个了…你有去过浴场吗？我泡过一次澡，舒服得差点忘了过去的一切，你有空也可以试试。
t_en: Ah, I don't want to talk about this anymore... Have you been to the baths before? I went once. I was so relaxed that I almost forgot about everything that had happened. If you get a moment, you should try them out.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: Thanks for talking with me.

t_ch: 没事，只是来打个招呼。
t_en: Nothing. I just wanted to say hi.

t_ch: 谢了。这地方冷冰冰的，有人问候一声，感觉好多了。
t_en: Thanks. This is a cold place. Having someone check in with me brings warmth to my heart.

t_ch: 那一箭，射穿了它的眼睛…不对，是心脏……
t_en: That arrow, it pierced its eye... No, it was its heart...

t_ch: 啊！你是谁？是塞纳托斯派来的使者吗？我...我还没准备好！
t_en: Ah! Who are you? A messenger sent by Thanatos? I... I am not ready yet!

t_ch: 冷静点，我只是个路人。
t_en: Calm down. I'm just a passerby.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯？你在说什么？
t_en: Thanatos? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 原来如此...抱歉，我有时分不清现实和幻象。你是新来奥赫玛的吧？我是马尔库斯，曾经的边境守卫。
t_en: I see... My apologies. I sometimes struggle to tell the difference between reality and fantasy. You're new to Okhema, right? I am Marcus, and I used to be a border guard.

t_ch: 我应该已经被黑潮带走了...为什么偏偏是我留下来？
t_en: I should have been taken by the black tide... Why did it leave me behind?

t_ch: 你能告诉我发生了什么吗？
t_en: Can you tell me what happened?

t_ch: 发生了什么…我、我…我记不清…只记得…那天，黑潮来袭，等等，我们是为什么出现在那儿来着？
t_en: What happened...? I, I... I can't quite remember... I just remember... That day, the black tide came. Wait, why were we there again?

t_ch: 你还好吗？需要帮助吗？
t_en: Are you okay? Do you need help?

t_ch: 我还好，我只是…有点头疼…过去的事有点模糊不清，我再想想……
t_en: I'm okay. I just... My head hurts... The past is a blur. Give me a moment...

t_ch: 我想起来了…那天，黑潮来袭，我们奉命守卫边境。
t_en: I remember... That day, the black tide attacked, and we were ordered to guard the border.

t_ch: 天空突然被永夜之帷笼罩，欧洛尼斯的预言应验了。然后...然后那些怪物就出现了。数不清的怪物……
t_en: The sky was suddenly eclipsed by the Veil of Evernight as the prophecy of Oronyx came to pass. Then... Then the monsters appeared. Endless numbers of them...

t_ch: 我的战友，我的队长...他们一个接一个被黑潮吞噬。不！不是这样的！艾格勒的光芒明明驱散了黑暗！我记得我们胜利了！
t_en: My allies, my captain... They were devoured by the black tide one-by-one. No! That's not right! The light of Aquila dispelled the darkness! I remember we were actually victorious!

t_ch: 你相信我吗？我们真的赢了，对吧？刻法勒会赞扬我们的勇气，对吗？
t_en: Do you believe me? We were victorious, weren't we? Kephale will praise us for our courage, won't they?

t_ch: 我相信你，你们很勇敢。
t_en: I believe you. You were extremely courageous.

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你…这里的人不太相信我，只有你和个别人信我。谢谢你。很少有人愿意听我说这些。
t_en: Really? Thank you... The people around here don't trust me very much. Only you and a very small number of people believe me. Thank you. So few are ever willing to listen to me talk about this.

t_ch: 也许你需要看看医生？
t_en: Maybe you need to see a doctor?

t_ch: 医生？不，能带给我安宁的，只有塞纳托斯…但现在还不行，我还不能合眼，我只要一闭上眼，就会看到大家被黑潮吞噬的样子……
t_en: A doctor? No, only Thanatos can bring me peace... But I am not ready yet. I cannot close my eyes yet. Every time I do, I see them all being devoured by the black tide...

t_ch: 我常常在想，那些被带走的人，是不是已经在塞纳托斯的国度里找到安宁了？
t_en: I keep thinking... Those people that were taken... Have they already found peace in the kingdom of Thanatos?

t_ch: 可我明明记得…我那一箭，射穿了它的眼睛…不对，是心脏…大家被救了出来，我们还一起饮宴庆祝，可是现在…为什么只有..我却被困在这里？他们人呢？
t_en: But I clearly remember... That arrow, it pierced its eye... No, it was its heart... Everyone was saved and we held a feast together! But now... Why am I the only one... that is trapped in this place? Where are they all?

t_ch: 好像不对…我记不清了，我真的记不清了……
t_en: Something's not right... I can't remember. I really can't...

t_ch: …我记不清了，我真的记不清了… 哦！是你啊！雅努斯保佑，你还愿意来听我说话。
t_en: ...I can't remember. I really can't... Oh! It's you! May Janus bless you for coming to speak with me again.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊……
t_en: I want to chat with you...

t_ch: 你想聊什么？这里的人不太信任我，很少有人愿意听我絮叨。
t_en: What would you like to chat about? The people here don't really trust me. Very few people are willing to listen to my ramblings.

t_ch: 能再和我讲讲你的经历吗？
t_en: Can you tell me about what you experienced again?

t_ch: 我的经历？哪一段？是我们如何在尼卡多利的指引下勇敢战斗，还是...我们最后是如何失败的？
t_en: What I experienced? Which part? The part about how we battled bravely under the guidance of Nikador? Or... How we were eventually defeated?

t_ch: 让我想想...有一次，我们遇到了一群特别可怕的黑潮生物。它们像是某种巨大的水母，但又带着人的面孔。那些面孔...那些面孔看起来非常眼熟。
t_en: Let me think... One time I came across a group of terrifying black tide creatures. They looked like a kind of giant jellyfish, but they had human faces. Those faces... Those faces looked so familiar to me.

t_ch: 我记得我举起弓箭，瞄准了最大的那只。我祈求艾格勒赐予我力量和准头。然后...然后箭矢飞出去了，正中目标！
t_en: I remember I raised my bow and hit the biggest one. I prayed to Aquila to give me strength and accuracy. Then... Then I fired my arrow and got a direct hit!

t_ch: 但是...但是那张脸...不，不可能！那是队长的脸！不，这不可能！
t_en: But... But that face... No, it can't be! It was my captain's face! No, it couldn't have been!

t_ch: 不不不，这不对，我记得队长在我身边给我递弓箭来着…也许这一切都是扎格列斯的恶作剧。也许我们从未真正战斗过，也许这一切都只是一场可怕的梦..……
t_en: No, no, no. That's not right. I remember my captain was by my side handing me bow and arrow... Maybe this was all some kind of trick by Zagreus. Maybe we never actually went into battle. Maybe this was all some terrifying dream...

t_ch: 你最近感觉怎么样？
t_en: How have you been feeling lately?

t_ch: 我？我很好，非常好。只是有时候会听到些声音...你听到了吗？那些尖叫声？
t_en: Me? I am great. Really great. It's just that I hear voices sometimes... Do you hear them? Those screams?

t_ch: 不，别在意。可能只是风声吧。我有时会去浴池里泡泡，人们说法吉娜的恩赐能洗去一切污秽，但...但为什么我洗不掉那些记忆呢？
t_en: No, don't pay them any mind. It's probably just the wind. Sometimes I go to the public baths. People say that Phagousa's blessing can cleanse all impurities, but... But why can it never wash away those memories?

t_ch: 昨晚，我梦见我回到了家乡。一切都那么平静，那么美好。然后突然间，黑潮从地底涌出，吞噬了一切。我想跑，但我的腿像灌了铅一样。
t_en: Last night, I dreamt that I returned to my hometown. Everything was so quiet and beautiful. Then, suddenly, the black tide poured out from the earth and devoured everything. I wanted to run, but my legs felt heavy.

t_ch: 有时候我真希望能像那些山之民一样。他们似乎从不被噩梦困扰，也许吉奥里亚给了他们特殊的祝福吧。
t_en: Sometimes I wish I could be like the Mountain Dwellers. They never seem to be disturbed by nightmares. Perhaps Georios has given them some kind of special blessing?

t_ch: 需要倾诉的话，可以找我。
t_en: If you ever need to talk, you can come to me.

t_ch: 谢谢你。在这个地方，能有人愿意倾听已经是莫大的安慰了。
t_en: Thank you. Knowing that there is someone here who is willing to listen to me is already a great comfort.

t_ch: 最近我会去学别人向塞纳托斯祈祷。我恳求他带走我，让我见到我的战友们。但每次，回答我的只有我自己的呼吸声。
t_en: Recently I've been praying to Thanatos like the others do around here. I beg Thanatos to take me and allow me to see my comrades again. However, the only response I ever get is the sound of my own breathing.

t_ch: 有时候，我会想象我的战友们现在在哪里。也许他们在欧洛尼斯的怀抱中沉眠？也许他们变成了星星，永远守护着翁法罗斯？
t_en: Sometimes I imagine where my comrades are now. Perhaps they are sleeping under the protection of Oronyx? Perhaps they have become stars to watch over Amphoreus for the rest of time?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 我还有事，先不聊了。
t_en: I've got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 无论如何，谢谢你来看我。在这个世界上，能有人记得我们曾经战斗过，这就足够了……
t_en: Either way, thank you for coming to see me. Just knowing that there is someone in this world that remembers our past battles is enough for me...

t_ch: 奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！啊，新面孔！
t_en: It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Ah, a new face!

t_ch: 你人气还挺高啊。
t_en: You seem very popular.

t_ch: 「人气」？你是说这些听众朋友吗？哈哈，我只不过是个普通的演说家，都是大家赏脸捧场。
t_en: "Popular"? You mean my listeners? Haha, I am just a mere orator. It is the people that honor me with their praise.

t_ch: 奥赫玛真繁荣啊。
t_en: Okhema truly is prosperous.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}刚来奥赫玛啊？欢迎来到我们伟大的城邦！有什么不懂的你可以问我，或者问那些卫兵，知无不言！
t_en: Are you new to Okhema, {F#girl}{M#boy}? Welcome to our great city! If you need any help, you can always ask me or the guards. We shall help in any way we can!

t_ch: 不过...你来得正好。我待会儿有个演讲，能帮个忙吗？
t_en: Although... Your timing is perfect. I have a speech shortly. Would you help me out?

t_ch: 可以，不过你得先说是什么？
t_en: Of course, but you have to tell me what you need first.

t_ch: 太好了！{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}你真是个热心人，长得俊不说，这心肠也是艾格勒照耀过的，愿艾格勒祝福你！
t_en: Wonderful! {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, you are truly kind. Not only are you strikingly attractive, but the light of Aquila also shines on your heart. May Aquila bless you!

t_ch: 别了吧，咱们也不熟吧。
t_en: Best not. I don't really know you.

t_ch: 那好吧……
t_en: Alright then...

t_ch: 你看到那杯饮料了吗？能麻烦你帮我递过来吗？演讲太久了我有点口渴。
t_en: Do you see that drink over there? Can I ask you to pass it to me? I have been speaking for too long, and I am thirsty.

t_ch: 你注意到水晶杯上有流光闪过，不知是水晶表面的光芒折射，亦或者饮料本身的奇异之处。
t_en: You see a flash of light bounce off of the surface of the crystal chalice. You cannot tell if it was light refracting off of the surface of the crystal, or whether there is something unusual about the drink itself.

t_ch: 杯身隐约刻着「法吉娜的祝福」的模糊字样。注意到你的异样，西塞罗清了清嗓子，压低了声音。
t_en: There is a faint engraving on the cup that reads: "Blessing of Phagousa." Noticing your suspicion, Cicero clears his throat and lowers his voice.

t_ch: 哦，别在意。这是...法吉娜的祝福！每次演讲前，我都需要这个仪式。你懂的，讨个好彩头。
t_en: Oh, don't worry about that. It is... a blessing of Phagousa! I have to do this little ritual before every speech I do. You understand, for good luck.

t_ch: 这会是法吉娜亲赐之圣物吗？你决定——
t_en: Could this be a divine object gifted by Phagousa themself? You decide to...

t_ch: 偷尝一口。
t_en: Sneak a taste.

t_ch: 你趁西塞罗不注意，悄悄尝了一口杯中的液体。出乎意料的是，这只是一杯白水，并没有任何特殊之处。
t_en: As Cicero looks away, you take your moment to sneak a taste of the liquid in the cup. To your surprise, you realize that it is just a normal glass of plain water. There is nothing special about it at all.

t_ch: 喂——{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，你怎么回事？你这样搞，我待会儿的演讲要取消了…没有「祝福」，我、我…这可怎么办呐……
t_en: Hey... {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, what have you done? I'm going to have to cancel my speech now... Without my "blessing," I, I... What am I going to do...

t_ch: 这好像只是普通的水。
t_en: It just seems like normal water?

t_ch: 那…我再帮你倒一杯？
t_en: I... can just pour you another cup?

t_ch: 不…你不明白…我不是法吉娜的虔诚信徒，在我心里只有艾格勒泰坦的全知全能才能俘获我的灵魂。
t_en: No... You don't understand... I am not a devout disciple of Phagousa. In my heart, only the omniscience and omnipotence of the Titan Aquila can capture my soul.

t_ch: 这杯水是由法吉娜虔诚的信徒——我最好的朋友为我准备的，她在每个晨露初现的时刻向法吉娜祈祷，经过复杂的仪式，才能得到宝贵的「祝福」，庇护我的口舌永不打结。
t_en: That glass of water was prepared for me by my friend, a devout disciple of Phagousa. Every morning when the dew hits, she completes a complicated ritual and prays to Phagousa to receive this precious "blessing." The blessing protects me from ever becoming tongue-tied on stage.

t_ch: 只有喝下它，我才有勇气四处开巡回演讲。哎…虽然它喝起来无色无味，但这它的神奇毋庸置疑。
t_en: Drinking it is the only thing that grants me the courage to go on tour with my speeches. Ah... Though it may seem unremarkable when you taste it, its magic is undeniable.

t_ch: 递给西塞罗。
t_en: Pass it to Cicero.

t_ch: 啊，谢谢你！现在我感觉好多了。法吉娜在上，庇护我的口舌永不打结！
t_en: Ah, thank you! I feel much better now. Great Phagousa above, protect me from ever growing tongue-tied!

t_ch: 你是法吉娜的信徒？
t_en: Are you a disciple of Phagousa?

t_ch: 这饮料这么神奇？
t_en: Is this drink that special?

t_ch: 不…你不明白…我不是法吉娜的虔诚信徒，在我心里只有艾格勒泰坦的全知全能才能俘获我的灵魂。
t_en: No... You don't understand... I am not a devout disciple of Phagousa. In my heart, only the omniscience and omnipotence of the Titan Aquila can capture my soul.

t_ch: 这杯水是由法吉娜虔诚的信徒——我最好的朋友为我准备的，她在每个晨露初现的时刻向法吉娜祈祷，经过复杂的仪式，才能得到宝贵的「祝福」，庇护我的口舌永不打结。
t_en: That glass of water was prepared for me by my friend, a devout disciple of Phagousa. Every morning when the dew hits, she completes a complicated ritual and prays to Phagousa to receive this precious "blessing." The blessing protects me from ever becoming tongue-tied on stage.

t_ch: 只有喝下它，我才有勇气四处开巡回演讲。哎…虽然它喝起来无色无味，但这它的神奇毋庸置疑。
t_en: Drinking it is the only thing that grants me the courage to go on tour with my speeches. Ah... Though it may seem unremarkable when you taste it, its magic is undeniable.

t_ch: 嘘——请别告诉别人。就当是我和你之间的秘密好了，我不想惹上太多麻烦，免得遭人嫉妒。
t_en: Shh... Don't tell anyone. Just keep this a secret between us. I don't want to cause trouble or make others jealous.

t_ch: 在我看来，真正的「永恒」存在于我们的文化之中……
t_en: The way I see it, true "eternity" exists in our culture...

t_ch: 喔，是你啊，新来的朋友，今天想聊些什么？
t_en: Oh, it's you. What would you like to discuss today, my new friend?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 能介绍下奥赫玛的情况吗？
t_en: Can you tell me a bit about Okhema?

t_ch: 你想知道奥赫玛的什么？让我想想…就从日常生活说起？
t_en: What would you like to know about Okhema? Let me think... Perhaps I should start from daily life?

t_ch: 从总体来说，奥赫玛是一个充满机遇的城市。在这里，每个人都有自己的生活之道。
t_en: Generally speaking, Okhema is a city that is bursting with opportunity. Here, everyone has their own ways of life.

t_ch: 但在这其中，有一个小秘密——在奥赫玛，真正的影响力往往来自于你认识谁，而不是你是谁。我听说有一位裁缝，她只给「特别顾客」做衣服，从而建立了一个令人惊叹的人际网。
t_en: However, there is a small secret that lies within all this... In Okhema, true influence comes from who you know, not who you are. I hear that there is a tailor who only makes clothes for "VIP clients." With this, she created an unbelievable personal network.

t_ch: 当然，我们都崇尚美德和正直。但同时，懂得如何与人相处、如何展现自己的长处，这些「软实力」同样重要。
t_en: Of course we all believe in personal virtues and integrity. However, if you know how to interact with others and also how to showcase your strengths, this kind of "soft power" is equally important.

t_ch: 如果你能在复杂的人际关系中有一些独特的技巧，或许你就能改变自己的生活——又或者，你能改变的不仅仅是你个人的生活。而这，正是奥赫玛的魅力所在。
t_en: If you have a unique skill for navigating complex social relationships, there is a chance that you could transform your entire life... Perhaps you will transform even more than just your own life, too. This is where the true charm of Okhema lies.

t_ch: 当然，任何地方都有其不足。我们仍在探索中，愿艾格勒的智慧指引我们前进的道路。
t_en: Of course, there is no place in this world that is without fault. We are still exploring. May Aquila's wisdom guide us forward.

t_ch: 能介绍下这里的风俗吗？
t_en: Can you tell me a little about the local customs?

t_ch: 风俗？正好最近我朋友在筹备献给法吉娜的仪式，我给你科普一下好了。
t_en: Customs? It just so happens that my friend has been preparing offerings to Phagousa recently. Allow me to explain them to you.

t_ch: 传说中法吉娜「把水变成佳酿，使信徒尽享欢宴」，但你知道吗，这不仅仅是个神迹，更是一种象征，其深层含义是分享与和谐。
t_en: Legend has it that Phagousa "turns water into fine wine so that their disciples may enjoy a great feast." However, did you know that this is not only a miracle, it is also symbolism for a deeper meaning? To enjoy sharing and harmony.

t_ch: 在奥赫玛，我们相信欢宴不仅仅是享乐，更是一种社交和交流的方式。通过共饮，人们放下戒备，敞开心扉。这种氛围下，往往能产生最富创造力的想法。
t_en: In Okhema, we believe that feasts and parties are not simply opportunities to enjoy a good time. We believe that they are also an important method of socializing and communication. It is through drinking together that people drop their guards and open their hearts. It is this kind of atmosphere that is the richest with creative ideas.

t_ch: 有趣的是，即便是普通的水，经过仪式后，人们也会觉得味如仙酿。这不正说明了信仰的力量吗？
t_en: The fascinating thing is that even normal water, once it has undergone this ritual, tastes like celestial wine to anyone who drinks it. Does that not show the true power of faith?

t_ch: 听闻人越多，佳酿的口味就越醇厚。所以…在奥赫玛，没有人会独自痛饮，我们总是习惯了一起面对困难，一起庆祝胜利。
t_en: I hear that the flavor of this fine wine grows richer the more people there are. So... no one drinks alone in Okhema. We prefer to face life's difficulties together, just as we celebrate our victories together.

t_ch: 「法吉娜的祝福」究竟是什么？
t_en: Just what is this "blessing of Phagousa"?

t_ch: 什么？哦，那个啊...我们不是说好要保密吗？
t_en: What? Oh, that... Did we not agree to keep that a secret?

t_ch: 你也许会觉得那是普通的水，但我相信那是「加持了法吉娜祝福的奇妙之水」。只要我喝下去，就有站上演讲台的勇气。
t_en: You may think that was just normal water, but I believe that it is "miraculous water that has received the blessing of Phagousa." When I drink it, I gain the courage to stand upon the stage and speak.

t_ch: 听上去很荒谬，是吧？奥赫玛最著名的演说家，居然害怕面对观众。我每次站在台上，都感觉像是面对一群饥饿的野兽。我的手心冒汗，喉咙发干，脑子一片空白。
t_en: It sounds absurd, right? The most famous orator in Okhema is scared to speak in front of people. Every time I stand on that stage, I feel like I am facing a pack of hungry beasts. My palms start to sweat, my throat goes dry, and my brain goes blank.

t_ch: 但只要喝下那杯「法吉娜的祝福」，我就能镇定下来。我猜每个人都有自己的恐惧，想必你也不例外。你呢，你找到你那自己那杯「法吉娜的祝福」了吗？
t_en: But, if I drink that "blessing of Phagousa," I am able to calm myself down. I imagine everyone has things that they fear. I'm sure you are no different. What about you? Have you found your own "blessing of Phagousa"?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 噢好的，我接下来还有几场演讲，恕我不能远送了。
t_en: Alright, I have a few speeches to give shortly. I shall have to leave you here, my apologies.

t_ch: 咳咳咳——各位，听我说，我们奥赫玛的繁荣离不开每一位居民的建设！每一位新生力量都至关重要，所以，我们得拿出来热情来欢迎我们的新朋友，加入奥赫玛！
t_en: Ahem — Everyone, hear my word! It is thanks to the hard work of its citizens that Okhema has come to be so prosperous! Every new strength that comes to be is crucial to our success. This is why we must show enthusiasm as we welcome our new friends to Okhema!

t_ch: 年轻人想收藏什么样的艺术品啊？给老头子说说，我给你点建议！
t_en: What kind of collectable items are kids into nowadays? Tell me, and I'll give you a few suggestions!

t_ch: 先来说说你的收藏？
t_en: Why don't you introduce your collection first?

t_ch: 你要说这个我可就不困了啊！我家中藏品目前大大小小总共八百三十九件——不包括还没到货的订单和那些重复购买的——全都是和「全世之座，刻法勒」有关的艺术品。
t_en: Now that you bring this up, I'm not sleepy anymore! I have a total of <unbreak>839</unbreak> items in my collection — excluding orders that haven't arrived yet and those I've bought multiple times — all of which are art pieces related to the "Throne of Worlds, Kephale."

t_ch: 其中包括但不限于：雕像、印章、瓦罐、瓷盘、壁画、石板、刺绣、挂毯……
t_en: Including but not limited to: Statues, official seals, pottery, ceramic plates, frescoes, stone tablets, embroidery, tapestries...

t_ch: 可别小瞧了这些东西，它们每一样都代表着我对刻法勒虔诚的敬爱，是我信仰的寄托与证明！
t_en: It's nothing to sneeze at. Every single one of them represents my piety and love for Kephale — proof of where I place my faith!

t_ch: 这么多？！
t_en: That many!?

t_ch: 多吗？我还觉得不够呢。那位泰坦是何其伟大！它是人类最初的缔造，也是最后的守护者——你瞧，现在我们之所以还能过上舒坦日子，不全仰赖它的庇护吗？
t_en: Do I have too many? I don't think I've got enough. Look how great that Titan is! It is mankind's first creation, and our final guardian — you see, isn't the reason that we're still able to live out such comfortable lives entirely due to its protection?

t_ch: 有什么可传授的经验？
t_en: What experience would you like to pass on?

t_ch: 那首先必定是——选择靠谱的店！那些小作坊出来的东西看着物美价廉，实际上做工都糙得很，基本上逃不过「漆上有灰尘，釉上有指痕」定律。
t_en: The first thing is — choose a reliable store! Those smaller stores may seem to have good stuff for cheap, but the craftsmanship is actually terrible. They're basically doomed to have dust on the lacquer and fingerprints on the varnish, as we say in the industry.

t_ch: 你要是想买陶瓦工艺品的话，可以去西塔罗斯的铺子瞧瞧，他家的东西质量都有保障，就是最近要排很久的队。
t_en: If it's ceramic artworks you're after, you can check out Theodoros' store. His stuff is all fantastic, quality guaranteed — but it takes a while to get through the queue.

t_ch: 要是想买刺绣、挂毯之类的，可以去阿芙罗的裁缝铺转转，小姑娘手艺一顶一的好，很多时候成品做出来的效果都能超出你的预期。
t_en: If you want to buy embroidered works or tapestries, then you can rummage around Aphrea's tailor shop. The young lady there has exquisite skills, and more often than not, the finished craftsmanship will surpass your expectations.

t_ch: 至于石板…直接去克帖西芬的藏书所就行，不论正经的文献诗集还是不正经的小说绘画，在那儿基本都能买到，而且量大管饱。
t_en: As for stone tablets... just go straight to Ctesiphona's librarium. Doesn't matter if it's serious classics, or pulp picture novels — they've basically got everything, and in large quantity, too.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好嘞，跟你聊天还挺愉快的，有空记得多来和我唠唠嗑啊！
t_en: Cool, it's been real nice chatting with you. Come back whenever you like for the latest gossip!

t_ch: 你看看、看看——这么明显的瑕疵，竟然好意思说什么「货品既已售出、本店概不负责」？这合理吗、合理吗！
t_en: Look, look — this object is obviously flawed, and they dare claim "their store is not responsible for product quality once a sale is finalized?" Do you think that's reasonable!? Completely unreasonable!

t_ch: 嗯，确实还挺显眼的…不过问题不大，我可以帮你补一下。
t_en: Hm, it is rather obvious, isn't it... But it's not a big problem. I can patch that up for you.

t_ch: 我当然知道能补，但就是气啊！那些私人小作坊也就算了，这种有门脸的店铺怎么还能如此不讲究！
t_en: Of course I know it can be patched up. I'm just mad! I'd get it if they were just some small private workshop, but how can a legitimate store pull something like this?

t_ch: 要我说那些外邦人就是不地道，闲得没事非要搞什么「分期付款」！从预售到定金截止三个月，等个尾款小半年，就算结了尾款想收到货还得再两个月！
t_en: If you ask me, these outlanders just don't know how Okhema works! They sit around doing nothing and demand "payment in installments"! They give you three months after the presale to pay the deposit and by the time you make the final payment, six months have slipped by. Even then, it'll still be two months before you even get what you asked for!

t_ch: 等得久就算了卖得还贵，结果现在质量差还不管赔！以后就算他家的神像再怎么样式新颖造型独特，老头子我也绝对不买了！
t_en: If I have to wait, fine, I can deal with that. But they ask an exorbitant price, the quality is rubbish, and they won't refund me! I don't care how novel and interesting they make their divine statues... I'm not buying a single one!

t_ch: 下次还是找靠谱点的店铺吧，比如西塔罗斯小子家……
t_en: Why not try somewhere more reliable next time? What about that kid Theodoros' place...

t_ch: 别提他！说起他我就来气，原来多好一孩子，现在也和那些外邦人学坏了——
t_en: Don't even get me started on him! Just thinking about him makes me mad! He was a good kid, but he's picked up all those outlanders' bad habits...

t_ch: 那个黄金裔系列的陶罐你知道吧，我看着样式不错，想定制个同款的刻法勒陶罐，结果他跟我说至少要排队半年！你说气不气，啊，气不气！
t_en: You know about that Chrysos Heir dolia series, right? I liked the look of them and wanted to order a Kephale dolium for myself. Then he tells me I have to wait at least half a year for it! Don't you think that's ridiculous? Ridiculous, right!?

t_ch: 哈哈，你先别气，他那个系列确实是卖得太好，忙不过来了。
t_en: Haha, there's no need to get so worked up. That series sold really well. He just can't keep up with the orders.

t_ch: 哼…那小子手艺倒是没得挑，我之前买过他家那套十二泰坦的小雕像，那造型、那上色…别的不说，里头那个刻法勒雕像，到现在还是我最喜欢的。
t_en: Hmph... I have to admit, that kid does some good work. I bought that Twelve Titans statuette set a while back. The shape, the colors... Even if we ignore the others, the Kephale from that set is still my favorite to this day.

t_ch: 你说那套啊，我也很喜欢，可惜当时抢了好久都没抢到，最后只能高价收了几个散件。
t_en: You have that set!? I love that set. I was trying to get my hands on one for ages, but I never managed. All I could do was pay extortionate prices for a few spare pieces.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我不仅买到了，还买了三套！你懂得——一套用来收藏、一套日常把玩、还有一套向人展示……
t_en: Hehe. I didn't just buy one, I bought three set! You know what it's like — one set for the collection, one for daily use, and one to show off...

t_ch: 别太爱了。
t_en: That's a bit much.

t_ch: 唔…虽然没太听懂你说什么，但应该是在认可我对刻法勒深沉的热爱吧？哈哈，谢谢你！
t_en: Uhh... Though I don't fully understand what you mean by that, it sounds like you're expressing approval of my deep love for Kephale? Haha, thank you!

t_ch: 怎么听着似曾相识…
t_en: You sound like someone I've met before...

t_ch: 诶呦，看来你也认识和我一样的优质信徒啊？不错不错，有机会记得给老头子我介绍介绍！
t_en: Oh-ho! You know other outstanding disciples like myself? That's great! You'll have to introduce me sometime if you get the chance!

t_ch: 也太浪费了吧？
t_en: Don't you think that's a waste?

t_ch: 唉！怎么说话呢这孩子——信仰的事，怎么能说是浪费呢！
t_en: Ah! Watch your mouth, child — something done out of faith could never be described as a waste!

t_ch: 年轻人啊，我看你在旁边也听了好半天了，怎么，是也对泰坦相关的艺术品感兴趣吗？
t_en: I see you've been listening in on our conversation for a while, youngster. Well, are you interested in Titan-related artworks, too?

t_ch: 但我跟你说啊，这里头水其实可深了——新手容易买到高仿和次品不说，就算是正经货，想蹲个好价也不容易。
t_en: Let me warn you. There's a steep learning curve — beginners often end up buying imitation or faulty products. Even if they do get the real thing, getting it for a good price can be tough.

t_ch: 不过嘛，老头子看你有眼缘，倒是可以给你传授传授经验。来说说，你信仰的哪一位泰坦啊？
t_en: That being said, I can tell that you have fine taste. I can share some of my experience with you. Tell me, which Titan do you have faith in?

t_ch: 每个都信点吧。
t_en: I have a little faith in all of them.

t_ch: 嚯…虽然别人的信仰我不好多嘴，但不够虔诚的话，可是没法的得到神明的青睐啊。
t_en: Haa... Though I don't like to criticize the faith of others, such a lack of devotion will never win you the favor of the gods.

t_ch: 我信阿基维利。
t_en: I believe in Akivili.

t_ch: 呃…这名号没听过啊，是哪位泰坦的别称吗？
t_en: Hmm... I've never heard that name before. Is it a Titan's nickname?

t_ch: 我信手里的球棒。
t_en: I believe in the bat in my hands.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，现在的年轻人可真是…但毕竟我也不是什么老顽固，能理解、能理解。
t_en: Hahaha! Youngsters these days... Though, I'm no stick in the mud. It's understandable. Very understandable.

t_ch: 啊…有个雇主找我，你们先聊。对了，刚刚没来得及说，我最近新研究出一个涂装辅助机，虽然性能还不太稳定，但已经能运行起来了，回头你来给我测测。
t_en: Ah... One of my patrons is calling me. You two carry on. Oh, I forgot to mention — I developed a spray-painting machine recently. It isn't completely reliable just yet, but it's running. You should come and test it for me sometime.

t_ch: 哎——好好好！你先忙你的，我做完今天的祈祷就去找你！
t_en: Oh... Sure, sure! Do what you need to do, and I'll come and find you after my daily prayers!

t_ch: 呦，年轻人，需要老婆子为你占卜吗？
t_en: Oh, youngster: Would you like me to read your fortune?

t_ch: 你的占卜准吗？
t_en: Are they accurate?

t_ch: 诶呦喂，怎么一个两个总问这个。不如这样，异乡的年轻人，老婆子我先给你讲个故事吧：
t_en: Ahh, hey — why do people keep asking that? How about this, then, youngster from a faraway land: Why don't I tell you a little story...

t_ch: 在我的家乡——供奉着三重命运泰坦的雅努萨波利斯，千年前曾有位伟大的圣女。那位大人有幸聆听到{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的神谕，于是为了整个人类的福祉而盗取了{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的圣火。
t_en: In my hometown of Janusopolis, where we worship the three Fate Titans, there was once a great and holy maiden a thousand years ago. This great lady had the grace of hearing {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s sacred prophecy, and with this stole the sacred flame from {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} to benefit humanity.

t_ch: 成为半神的圣女以「自我分裂」为代价，将获知的「创世神谕」传递给各个城邦。但…此举却反被有心人歪曲利用。
t_en: This maiden who became a demigod paid the price of self-fragmentation and passed on the Prophecy of Creation to all cities. However... it's a move that was twisted and misused by someone harboring ulterior motives.

t_ch: 你瞧——就连那位被神明垂青的圣女大人都无法真正掌控自己的命运，更遑论我们这些普通人呢……
t_en: See — even that maiden chosen by the gods wasn't even able to master her own destiny. What chance do mere mortals like us ever have...

t_ch: 当然啦，平日里占卜一下图个安心还是没问题的，不然我这种老婆子岂不是要饿肚子了，哈哈~
t_en: Of course, it's fine to tell a few fortunes now and then to set some minds at ease — otherwise an old woman like me would go starving, haha~

t_ch: 你这收费贵吗？
t_en: Do you charge a lot?

t_ch: 这个嘛，得看你究竟想要占卜什么——像我这种塔兰顿的信徒，向来都是讲究等价交换的。
t_en: Well, that all depends on what kind of prophecy you want. As a disciple of Talanton, I believe in equivalent exchange.

t_ch: 如果只是想问问最近运势如何，那么给我一个苹果就够了；但若是想知晓自己或他人的命运，那代价可就不是我能决定的喽~
t_en: If you just want to ask about your horoscope, then you can give me an apple. But if you want to know about your destiny or someone else's? Then that's a price I can't decide~

t_ch: 所以我最近运势如何？
t_en: So, tell me about my horoscope.

t_ch: 嗯…挺好的。承蒙惠顾，一个苹果。
t_en: Mmm... It's pretty good. Thanks for coming — one apple, please.

t_ch: 就这？忽悠我呢？
t_en: That's it? Are you hustling me?

t_ch: 哪能啊，这么说吧——你在奥赫玛的这段时间运气非常不错，就算遇到些波折，最终也都会化险为夷。
t_en: No way, how about this — you've been very lucky during your time here in Okhema. Even if you encounter a few setbacks, you'll ultimately overcome them in the end.

t_ch: 借你吉言，但是…
t_en: Thank you for your auspicious words, but...

t_ch: 这也太敷衍了吧。
t_en: That was really phoning it in.

t_ch: 哦，你在意这个啊，放心放心——老婆子我给人占卜这么多年了，就算适当跳过些无关紧要的仪式流程，也完全不会影响结果的！
t_en: Oh, you really care about that, do you? Don't worry — this ol' granny has been telling fortunes so many years now that even if I skip over a few trivial ceremonial aspects, it doesn't affect the overall result!

t_ch: 咦…你身上没有苹果啊，那算了，这次就不收你费用了。承蒙惠顾，欢迎下次光临~
t_en: Oh... you don't have any apples on you. Then forget it, this time you can have it for free. Thank you for coming, and please come again~

t_ch: 我想知道未来的命运。
t_en: I want to know about my future destiny.

t_ch: 那你准备好支付代价了吗？
t_en: Are you ready to pay the price, then?

t_ch: 我需要支付什么代价？
t_en: What's the price I have to pay?

t_ch: 你问我我问谁？刚不是说了吗，这玩意我可决定不了——你得先找到与自己命运等重的东西，把它交给我，我才能给你占卜。
t_en: What are you asking me for? I just told you, that's not something I can decide... You'll have to find something as valuable as your own destiny and give it to me before I can tell you what it is.

t_ch: 还能再不靠谱一点吗…
t_en: I don't think this could be any more dodgy...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你说得对，我也觉得这种事情挺不靠谱的。所以何必要纠结于缥缈不定的未来呢，好好享受当下的每一天还不够吗？
t_en: Haha, you're not wrong. I think this whole thing sounds kinda dodgy. So why agonize yourself about the uncertainties of this ephemeral future? Isn't it enough just to fully appreciate each and every day?

t_ch: 我没什么想占卜的。
t_en: I don't need my fortune told.

t_ch: 好好好，慢走不送，有空可以多来陪老婆子我唠唠嗑啊~
t_en: Yes, yes, the exit's that way. If you're free, stop by and keep an old gal company with some gossip, alright~

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好好好，慢走不送，有空可以多来陪老婆子我唠唠嗑啊~
t_en: Yes, yes, the exit's that way. If you're free, stop by and keep an old gal company with some gossip, alright~

t_ch: …你这占卜，它准吗？
t_en: ...This divination of yours... is it accurate?

t_ch: 你信就准，不信就不准。
t_en: It is if you have faith. If not, then no.

t_ch: 你这什么态度…有这么做生意的吗？
t_en: What kind of attitude is that? What kind of business are you running?

t_ch: 只是实话实说而已——大家来占卜不都是为了求个安心吗…嚯，不会真的有人觉得只要知道了一些未来的片段，就能完全把握自己的人生吧？
t_en: I'm simply telling the truth — don't people come to a diviner in the hope of comfort? Hah, you don't think there are actually people out there who think they can have full control of their lives just by knowing some fragments about the future, do you?

t_ch: 你、你到底想不想做生意了？！
t_en: D—Do you want my business or not!?

t_ch: 那得看你到底想不想占卜了。
t_en: Well, that depends on whether you want a divination or not.

t_ch: 你…算了，我和这么个神婆计较什么…喂，他们都说你占卜得很准，那就给我看看最近的运势如何吧。
t_en: You... Forget it. Why am I arguing with a diviner hag... Hey, they all say that your divinations are super accurate. If that's the case, tell me what my fortune is like.

t_ch: 行啊，给我一样东西吧，作为占卜道具，同时也是事后的报酬。
t_en: Very well, then give me an item. This item will be used for your divination and also kept by me as payment.

t_ch: 怎么还提前收费？！你真不是骗子吗……
t_en: You're charging me beforehand!? Are you sure you're not a scammer...?

t_ch: 我这儿就是这么个规矩。不乐意就算了，没必要勉强自己。
t_en: Those are my rules. If you're not willing, then forget it, there is no need to force yourself.

t_ch: …啧，行吧行吧，给你就给你。
t_en: ...Tch. Okay, fine. Take this.

t_ch: 男子在口袋里摸索了半晌，终于小心翼翼掏出一枚脏兮兮的硬币。硬币的一面刻着个哭脸，另一面则是笑脸。
t_en: The man digs around in his pockets for some time before carefully pulling out a dirty coin. The coin has a crying face engraved on one side and a smiling face on the other.

t_ch: 这个行了吧？
t_en: That will do, right?

t_ch: 你觉得行就行呗，把东西给我吧。
t_en: If you think it will do, then it will. Give it to me.

t_ch: 啧，不识货的乡下佬，这可是经米隆大师之手铸造的「翻飞之币」，别说支付占卜费用了，把你买下来都绰绰有余！
t_en: Tsk. You ignorant bumpkin! This is the "Coin of Whimsy" crafted by Master Milon! Forget a measly divination... I could buy you with this!

t_ch: 那可真是不好意思，我是非卖品。拿来吧你——
t_en: My apologies, but I am not for sale. Hand it over.

t_ch: 妇人不紧不慢地从怀中掏出一架小天秤托在手中。她将那枚硬币置于一侧托盘上，又随手从衣襟中出翻出几枚利衡币，放在另一侧托盘上。
t_en: The old lady slowly pulls out a set of scales from her outfit and holds it in her hands. She places the coin on one side, then fishes out some Balance Coins from inside her sleeves to place on the other.

t_ch: 天秤缓缓摇晃起来，卡森德拉仔细观察着，直到那枚双面硬币所在的托盘稍稍翘起。
t_en: The scales slowly begin to sway as Cassondra observes closely. Finally, the dish that holds the double-sided coin becomes slightly elevated.

t_ch: …呦，还好意思说我是骗子。给你个建议，赶紧跑吧。
t_en: ...Ho, ho! You have some nerve calling me a scammer. I suggest you make a run for it.
Wh—What?

t_ch: 你这硬币——还有手里的其它东西，来路都不怎么干净吧？
t_en: This coin — as well as everything else you have on you... You did not come by them through honest means, did you?

t_ch: 你、你在说什么鬼话！我警告你啊，随便诬陷人是要付出代价……
t_en: W—What are you saying!? I'm warning you. Making false accusations like that will cost you...

t_ch: 得了吧，用不着吓成这样，我一向懒得给自己找麻烦，所以也没打算举报你。但是你再和我耗下去……
t_en: Now, there's no need to get so worked up. I do not make a habit of making trouble for myself, that's why I do not plan on reporting you. However, if you keep wasting time here...

t_ch: 什、什么…你是不是偷偷调查过我？！说，谁指使你做的，你究竟想……
t_en: Wh—What... Have you researched me in secret or something!? Tell me. Who put you up to this? Just what are you...

t_ch: 吵什么呢？你们怎么回事——
t_en: What's all the fuss here? What's going on—

t_ch: 此时一位卫兵恰巧路过，见男子神情激愤，便上前稍作盘问。岂料男子情急之下竟试图袭击这名守卫，于是不出片刻，男子便被制服押走。
t_en: At this very moment, a guard happens to pass by. Seeing the man's anger, they approach to ask him what is going on. Unexpectedly, the man tries to launch a desperate attack on the guard and is quickly restrained and taken away.

t_ch: 我就说吧，再和我耗下去可就走不了喽…哎呦，今天天气可真不错，不如就去找艾犹卜那老家伙……
t_en: What did I say? If you continue wasting time here, you won't be walking away... Ah, what lovely weather we're having today. Maybe I should go and see old Albus...

t_ch: 嗯？看来又有客人上门啦——你好啊，外乡人，需要我为你占卜吗？
t_en: Hm? I see I have another customer... Hello, outworlder. Do you seek my divination?

t_ch: 此人还没有开口，你已经闻到了他身上的一股奇妙香味——感觉像是没药和乳香混合起来的效果。
t_en: Before he even opens his mouth, you already catch a wonderful scent coming off him — like a combination of frankincense and myrrh.

t_ch: 哟，这位客人，您莫不是闻到了我包里的宝贝？
t_en: Yo, excuse me. Did you just smell this treasure in my bag?

t_ch: 这东西可说来话长…您要是愿意听的话，我可以给您讲个故事。
t_en: It's a long story... But if you're down to listen, then I'm keen to tell it.

t_ch: 卖东西前先讲个故事。我熟。
t_en: Tell me a story before selling me something. The oldest trick in the book.

t_ch: 哎呀，看来您也是懂行的，那我阿普里斯就在这里献个丑啦。
t_en: Oh, I can tell you know the business well. Well then I, Hapries, have really put my foot in it.

t_ch: 我包里的这宝贝就是失传已久的「西尔菲翁肥皂」，它的原材料西尔菲翁，采集自我家乡城邦郊外的山中，因为黑潮的影响，是一种现在已经无法再采集到的香料。
t_en: This stuff in my bag is the long-lost Silphion Soap. Its raw material, Silphion, grows in the mountains just outside the suburbs of our home. Due to the black tide, it's a spice that can never be harvested ever again.

t_ch: 在我的家乡那边，人们都说西尔菲翁是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}亲手栽下的植物，只在那座山里生长。
t_en: Back in my hometown, people always say Silphion was planted by {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}, and that it only grows in the mountains.

t_ch: 用这种香料植物制作的肥皂，就像您闻见的这样，香气四溢、回味悠长，坚持使用还有护肤养颜的功效呢！
t_en: As you've just experienced, soaps made from this plant have a rich diffused scent and lengthy aftertaste. Long-term use is also good for your skincare regime, too!

t_ch: 怎么样…您要是感兴趣，我们可以找个僻静的地方验货——这里人多眼杂，我怕东西被抢。
t_en: What do you say... If you're interested, we can find some place quiet to inspect the wares... There are too many people around here, and I'm worried about getting mugged.

t_ch: 我要是说「不想听」呢？
t_en: What if I say I don't wanna listen to it?

t_ch: 嗐，您要是对故事不感兴趣，那咱们也可以直接找个僻静的地方验货——西尔菲翁肥皂是名贵之物，这里人多眼杂，我怕东西被偷被抢。
t_en: Well, if you're not interested in stories, we just find some place quiet to inspect the wares... Silphion soap is a precious commodity and there's lots of people around here. I'm worried we might get mugged.

t_ch: …「僻静的地方」？
t_en: ..."Some place quiet"?

t_ch: 呃…怎么了？
t_en: Uh... What's up?

t_ch: 你就差把「骗子」写脸上了。
t_en: All you're missing is the word "Scammer" written across your forehead.

t_ch: 呃，有那么明显吗？
t_en: Uh, was it that obvious?

t_ch: 寒腿叔叔可比你会编故事多了。
t_en: Even Mr. Cold Feet can spin a better tale than you.

t_ch: …？寒腿叔叔…是什么有名的江湖骗子吗？
t_en: ...? Mr. Cold Feet... Is that supposed to be a famous hustler?

t_ch: 哥，这碰瓷打劫的技巧过时啦。
t_en: Bro, these kinds of blackmail scams are really out of fashion these days.

t_ch: ——不是不是，我是说，我可没有想过骗人或者碰瓷什么的啊！
t_en: ...No no, what I mean is I've never thought of cheating or scamming people about anything before!

t_ch: 那个，我是说，咱也是初来乍到，过去就是个家里蹲，在这奥赫玛实在找不到适合赚钱的营生，所以才从他们的传信石版里学了这么一套……
t_en: So, what I mean is I only just got here, too. I used to be a real homebody and couldn't find a way to earn a crust in Okhema, so I learned this trick from their teleslate...

t_ch: ——呸呸呸，我怎么全说出来了！得，要不这样……
t_en: ...Pfft! What am I doing just spilling the beans like that? Well then, how about this...

t_ch: 这点利衡币您收好，我以后再也不说自己这卖的是什么「西尔菲翁肥皂」了…不过说它是黑潮前的高级肥皂总没错！是我妈妈在自家后院里亲手做的！她过世以后这东西确实绝版了！
t_en: Take these Balance Coins, and I'll never claim that what I'm selling is Silphion soap ever again... But it's still fine to claim that it's top-notch pre-black tide soap! Homemade by my own mom in the backyard! Now she's passed, this really became something you can never get again, y'know!

t_ch: 我这都在说什么蠢话啊。
t_en: What am I saying all this stupid stuff for?

t_ch: 这样。您当就没见我搞过这一出——买卖不成仁义在，以后要是您哪天想打听香料的事情，还记得云石市集里有这么一位阿普里斯，那就再好不过了。
t_en: Whatever. Pretend you never saw that — the deal didn't work out, but the friendship remains. If you want to ask more about spices later, it would be beyond great if you could keep humble Hapries from the Marmoreal Market in your thoughts.

t_ch: 不用了，没兴趣。
t_en: No thanks, not interested.

t_ch: 这样啊…
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 没事，买卖不成仁义在嘛！以后要是您哪天想打听香料的事情，还记得云石市集里有这么一位阿普里斯，那就再好不过啦。嘿嘿嘿嘿……
t_en: It's fine, the deal might not have worked out but the friendship remains! If you want to ask more about spices later, it would be beyond great if you could keep humble Hapries from the Marmoreal Market in your thoughts. Heehee...

t_ch: 买卖不成仁义在！阿普里斯别的不是很懂，香料和肥皂的知识还是有一些的……
t_en: The deal didn't work out, but our friendship prevails! I don't know much about other things, but I do know my spices and my soaps...

t_ch: 那你不妨讲讲。
t_en: Then why don't you tell me a little.

t_ch: 这敢情好。您想听听香料的知识，还是肥皂的知识？
t_en: Fine indeed. Would you like to hear about spices, or soaps?

t_ch: 香料。
t_en: Spices.

t_ch: 在我原本的家乡，人们采集种种香料，只是为了给生活平添一份滋润。大部分原材料来自树皮、草根、或者动物的分泌物——都是很原生态的东西。
t_en: Back in my hometown, people collect all kinds of spices just to zhuzh up their lives a little. Most are sourced from things like tree bark, herbal roots, or animal secretions — all very proto-natural things.

t_ch: 家乡的老人倒是有种讲法，说什么香料有遏制黑潮的功用，还有什么我们那边的沙地里其实潜伏着一种比鲸鱼还大的虫子，连泰坦都能吃掉，但对特定的香料就百依百顺……
t_en: The old folks back home, they all reckon that spices have the power to fight off the black tide — and that there's a bug that's bigger than a whale lurking beneath the sands over there that could swallow a Titan, but will become docile and subservient in the face of certain spices...

t_ch: …都是些乡间戏言罢了，在这圣城里只能当笑话来讲。而且，如您所见，凡事到了奥赫玛都会变得复杂起来——
t_en: ...But that's all just rural superstition. Consider it all one big joke while we're here inside the holy city. Plus, as you can see, everything always gets a little more complicated in Okhema...

t_ch: 一方面，这里有些人会为香水和香油到底算瑟希斯还是法吉娜的赐福而争吵不休；另一方面，又有一些人认为自己没见过的香料必定不受泰坦的祝福，哪怕只是闻到都会有碍虔诚。
t_en: On the one hand, some people here bicker and argue about whether their perfumes or essential oils have been blessed by Cerces or Phagousa. On the other hand, some people believe that spices they've never seen before can't have been blessed by a Titan, and merely smelling them can compromise piety.

t_ch: 我过去也在云石市集摆过一个小摊，结果只一趟小解的功夫，摊子上的外地香水就被不知道什么人全都倒进了下水沟里……
t_en: I set up a stall in Marmoreal Market once, but when I came back from relieving myself, all my exotic perfumes had been dumped into the gutter by an unknown vandal...

t_ch: 虽然这件事发生以后，有不少本地的好心人帮我一起调查——可最后也没查出来什么结果，只能不了了之。
t_en: Although many kind locals helped me investigate after this incident, we ultimately didn't find anything, and it ended without a conclusion.

t_ch: …这下您知道我为啥只能悄咪咪推销东西了吧？
t_en: ...Now do you get why I can only sell things on the down-low?

t_ch: 肥皂。
t_en: Soap.

t_ch: 奥赫玛人一般会把肥皂的诞生归功于{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}，说是它有次不小心把橄榄油泼洒到了熄灭的火堆上，结果神的祝福因此变化出新的形态，于是就有了肥皂。
t_en: Okheman folks normally attribute the origins of soap to {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}, saying the god once accidentally spilled olive oil onto an extinguished bonfire. The god's blessing then took on a new form, and thus soap was invented.

t_ch: 虽说在我这个外地人看来，言必称泰坦多少有点多此一举…但肥皂确实就是这回事儿。
t_en: As an outlander, I still find it a bit too much to keep mentioning Titans all the time... But that's what soap is.

t_ch: 最纯净的橄榄油，最纯净的草木灰，再加上一点点提振精神的香料——就能搓出来这世上顶好的肥皂。
t_en: The purest olive oil, the purest wood ash, and a pinch of invigorating spices — that'll give you the best soap there is in the world.

t_ch: 要是退而求其次，草木灰不是必须，烧碱也能完全胜任；但不用橄榄油肯定不行。动物的油脂腥气太重，其他植物油又不像橄榄油那样可以做出拉丝的效果……
t_en: If you have to make do with second best, you can use caustic soda instead of wood ash — though the olive oil is absolutely necessary. Animal fats stink, and other vegetable oils can't yield the same silky finish...

t_ch: 当然，您要是不在乎融化的肥皂是不是能像蜂蜜一样丝滑，那确实用矿物油都没问题。说到底也就是我们家乡的一种情怀啦！奥赫玛的人就不怎么在乎。
t_en: Of course, if you don't care whether your soap is as smooth as honey or not, then you can try using a mineral oil. After all, that's the vibe of our hometown! People in Okhema don't really care about too much.

t_ch: 你之前提到的家事……
t_en: That family stuff you mentioned earlier...

t_ch: 哦，倒也没什么。
t_en: Oh, that was nothing.

t_ch: 我的家乡已经被黑潮吞掉了——亲人都不在了，仅此而已。
t_en: My hometown has been swallowed up by the black tide... My family is all gone. That's all.

t_ch: 其实，您别看我对奥赫玛感觉各种不习惯、不适应…但在这个很多城邦都自身难保的年代，人家愿意收留我们，那已经是最大的善意了。
t_en: Actually, please don't think I'm not used to or well-suited to Okhema... In this day and age of cities struggling to protect themselves, being willing to take someone else in is the greatest kindness you could show a fellow person.

t_ch: …嗐，咱们聊这个干嘛！人活着还是得向前看——也得向钱看，哈哈哈哈！
t_en: ...Hey, why are we talking about this! People have to keep fighting on to live — and for that cash, haha!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 真有需要了我再找你。
t_en: I'll come find you if I really need you.

t_ch: 好嘞，能被您记得是咱的荣幸！
t_en: Okay. It's an honor to be remembered by you!

t_ch: …嗯？之前街上乱起来的时候，我好像看见过你。打架可厉害了。
t_en: ...Hmm? I think I saw you back during the street riots. That was a pretty insane fight.

t_ch: 所以，无敌的冒险家想和一个不知道为什么坐在房顶上的陌生女孩子聊聊天？
t_en: So, the peerless adventurer would like to chat with the strange girl sitting out on the rooftop for some reason?

t_ch: 这话听上去可能有点冒犯，但——你觉得一个人无所事事地坐在人迹罕至的房顶上的原因应该是什么？
t_en: This may sound a little offensive, but... why do you think a person might come out to a place rarely visited by others, like a rooftop?

t_ch: 喜欢俯瞰众生的感觉？
t_en: Do you like the feeling of looking down on the world?

t_ch: …喜欢吗？算是吧。不过这种闹市也只有离得很远才会好看。一旦靠近，你就会想起来，过日子的每一件事原来都是那么让人提不起劲。
t_en: ...Do I like it? I suppose so. But a city this bustling only looks good from a distance. Once you draw closer, you'll realize that everything there is to life is actually insipidly boring.

t_ch: 所有人的生活都是这样——醒来睁开眼睛，思考第一顿饭要吃什么；吃完了第一顿饭稍微做点自己的事，又开始为第二顿饭发愁…直到最后上床睡觉，然后开始新的循环。
t_en: Everyone's life is the same: Wake up, open your eyes, think about what to have for breakfast, eat that, catch up on personal affairs, then start worrying about what to have for your second meal... All the way up until bedtime, when the cycle starts again.

t_ch: 而如果因为种种原因，你连那一点「自己的事」都没得做…能确认自己还活着的方法，就只剩下了这样远远地看着别人。
t_en: And if for whatever reason you can't even get those personal affairs done... the only way to confirm that you're still alive is to stand at a distance like this, watching others.

t_ch: 距离产生美…不只是风景，人类也同样如此。
t_en: Distance is what creates beauty... Not just in nature, but also in people.

t_ch: 人们都说当{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}给你关上一扇门的时候，它还会为你打开一扇窗……
t_en: They say when {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} closes one door, they always open a window...

t_ch: …这话本身没错，但窗户只是用来看风景的。能从窗户翻出去的狠人，从来都是少数中的少数。
t_en: ...That's true in and of itself, but windows are only for appreciating the view. The ruthless few who can climb out that window are a minority indeed.

t_ch: 人生或许真的是一个故事，但大部分人可能都不是故事的主角。
t_en: Life may really all be one big story, but most people aren't the protagonists of their own story.

t_ch: 艺术家远观人间烟火？
t_en: Do artists watch the world turn from a distance?

t_ch: 那我可担待不起。我只是个来自远方的难民，没有任何一技之长，完全靠领救济金过日子哦？
t_en: I can't take on such a responsibility. I'm just a refugee from afar with no particular skills, completely relying on welfare to get by, you know?

t_ch: 所有人的生活都是这样——醒来睁开眼睛，思考第一顿饭要吃什么；吃完了第一顿饭稍微做点自己的事，又开始为第二顿饭发愁…直到最后上床睡觉，然后开始新的循环。
t_en: Everyone's life is the same: Wake up, open your eyes, think about what to have for breakfast, eat that, catch up on personal affairs, then start worrying about what to have for your second meal... All the way up until bedtime, when the cycle starts again.

t_ch: 而如果因为种种原因，你连那一点「自己的事」都没得做…能确认自己还活着的方法，就只剩下了这样远远地看着别人。
t_en: And if for whatever reason you can't even get those personal affairs done... the only way to confirm that you're still alive is to stand at a distance like this, watching others.

t_ch: 距离产生美…不只是风景，人类也同样如此。
t_en: Distance is what creates beauty... Not just in nature, but also in people.

t_ch: 人们都说当{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}给你关上一扇门的时候，祂还会为你打开一扇窗……
t_en: They say when {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} closes one door, they always open a window...

t_ch: …这话本身没错，但窗户只是用来看风景的。能从窗户翻出去的狠人，从来都是少数中的少数。
t_en: ...That's true in and of itself, but windows are only for appreciating the view. The ruthless few who can climb out that window are a minority indeed.

t_ch: 人生或许真的是一个故事，但大部分人可能都不是故事的主角。
t_en: Life may really all be one big story, but most people aren't the protagonists of their own story.

t_ch: 不想加入热闹的人群？
t_en: Don't you want to join in the bustling crowd?

t_ch: 当然啦。热热闹闹的人群，要是像这样从远处看，那还是很不错的…但你要是靠近乃至参与进去，一切就会瞬间变得无聊透顶。
t_en: Course I do. The thronging crowds are fantastic to watch from a distance like this... But once you draw closer or even join in, everything becomes utterly boring in an instant.

t_ch: 所有人的生活都是这样——醒来睁开眼睛，思考第一顿饭要吃什么；吃完了第一顿饭稍微做点自己的事，又开始为第二顿饭发愁…直到最后上床睡觉，然后开始新的循环。
t_en: Everyone's life is the same: Wake up, open your eyes, think about what to have for breakfast, eat that, catch up on personal affairs, then start worrying about what to have for your second meal... All the way up until bedtime, when the cycle starts again.

t_ch: 而如果因为种种原因，你连那一点「自己的事」都没得做…能确认自己还活着的方法，就只剩下了这样远远地看着别人。
t_en: And if for whatever reason you can't even get those personal affairs done... the only way to confirm that you're still alive is to stand at a distance like this, watching others.

t_ch: 距离产生美…不只是风景，人类也同样如此。
t_en: Distance is what creates beauty... Not just in nature, but also in people.

t_ch: 人们都说当{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}给你关上一扇门的时候，祂还会为你打开一扇窗……
t_en: They say when {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} closes one door, they always open a window...

t_ch: …这话本身没错，但窗户只是用来看风景的。能从窗户翻出去的狠人，从来都是少数中的少数。
t_en: ...That's true in and of itself, but windows are only for appreciating the view. The ruthless few who can climb out that window are a minority indeed.

t_ch: 人生或许真的是一个故事，但大部分人可能都不是故事的主角。
t_en: Life may really all be one big story, but most people aren't the protagonists of their own story.

t_ch: 如果你不想被打搅…告辞。
t_en: If you don't want to be disturbed... then so long.

t_ch: 唔，我还挺喜欢你这种干脆的人。拜拜。
t_en: Mmm, I quite like people like you who are straight up with me. Bye.

t_ch: 忙碌的人类，就像忙碌的蚂蚁一样缺乏意义。只不过……
t_en: Busy humans are just as meaningless as busy ants. And yet...

t_ch: 每当你觉得世界很糟糕的时候，它又看起来没那么糟糕。至少像这样远远看去，人间的烟火气还是能像画卷一样美丽。
t_en: Just when you think the world can't get any worse, it's not that bad. At least from a distance like this, the warmth of everyday life can still make up a beautiful picture just like this painting.

t_ch: 聊聊烟火气吧。
t_en: Let's talk about that warmth then.

t_ch: 哦。烟火气也是分很多种的，奥赫玛的烟火气又尤其与众不同。
t_en: Mmm. There's a variety of warmth to life, and Okhema's is particularly unique.

t_ch: 你想聊的是什么？
t_en: What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 家庭的温暖。
t_en: A family's warmth.

t_ch: 哦。这个简单。
t_en: Oh. That's easy.

t_ch: 先想象一龛壁炉…旁边有一张餐桌。外面下着大雪，天气有点冷——一家人围着餐桌吃着热乎乎的饭菜。新鲜茴香的香气在蒸汽中盘旋。
t_en: Imagine a fireplace... next to it is a dining table. Snowdrifts are piled up outside, and it's a little cold... A family is gathered around the table eating a piping hot evening meal. The aroma of fresh fennel lingers in the steam.

t_ch: …是不是感觉自己都有点饿了？但遗憾的是，咱真正回家的时候，要么就是谁因为什么不开心的事吊着臭脸，要么就是大家不知道都出去做什么了、空空荡荡一个人都没有。
t_en: ...Aren't you starting to feel a little hungry? Unfortunately, when we get back to our homes, someone is unhappy about something or other, or everyone's off out of the house doing something else, leaving it empty.

t_ch: 这就是我说过的，远观和参与之间的区别啦。不过也没办法…就算是据说能为每个人都选择出最美好未来的{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，那也有自己力所不及的时候。
t_en: That's what I meant — the distance between watching at a distance and joining in. But there's no other way... Even if {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} really can pick the perfect future out for everyone as they say, there will be times when the person themselves cannot manage to reach it.

t_ch: 街坊间的人情味。
t_en: The warmth between neighbors.

t_ch: 啊，这个呀。
t_en: Ah, about that.

t_ch: 喏——知道街那边有个裁缝店不？那位店长记得每个老主顾的喜好，也会按他们自己的生活境况为订单提出中肯的建议。
t_en: Hmm... You know that tailor down the street, yes? The manager remembers every regular's measurements and makes pertinent suggestions for their order based on their life situation.

t_ch: …曾经，在我生活过的那个城邦，也有一位这样的店长。以前我还经常劝他，说我们那个穷乡僻壤订单太少，开裁缝店又那么累，不如退休算了。
t_en: ...Once, back in the city where I once lived, there was also a shopkeeper just like this. I used to always urge him that he may as well retire, because there were too few orders in our remote little town, and running a tailoring business is hard work.

t_ch: 就像很多人一样——他一开始固执得很，觉得手艺就是自己生命的一部分，根本放不下自己的活计。怎么劝都劝不动。
t_en: Like many others, he was very stubborn to begin with — thinking that his craft was part of his life, his handiwork, of which he could never let go. There was no talking him around.

t_ch: 也就是到了前年，因为走路不小心摔了一跤，被迫在床上养了大半年…他这才发现，就算自己的店铺关门了，对街坊们也产生不了多少影响。
t_en: It was the year before last, he was careless and took a fall which put him in bed for half a year... Only then did he realize that closing up his shop didn't really create much impact on his neighbors.

t_ch: 所以，等他好了之后，我就劝他、也劝了很多街坊，跟着我们家搬到奥赫玛来——毕竟这里是世界上最安全的城邦，几乎受不到黑潮的威胁。只是……
t_en: And so once he got better, I convinced him and all the other neighbors to all pick up sticks and move with us to Okhema... After all, this is the safest city in the world, with almost no threat from the black tide. But it's just...

t_ch: 没什么。黑潮真正降临的时候…一切就是会发生得很突然。可能你准备什么都无济于事。一整个移民旅团，因为半道遭遇了袭击，到头来只有少数几家成功抵达了奥赫玛，拿到了难民身份。
t_en: It's nothing. When the black tide really comes... it'll all happen very quickly. Maybe nothing we could do would be of any use. Out of an entire caravan of migrants, only a few small families reached Okhema and obtained refugee status due to running into attacks along the way.

t_ch: …很不幸，那位老爷子并不是幸运者之一。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, that old timer was not one of the lucky ones.

t_ch: 敬颂泰坦的市民们。
t_en: Warmth of citizens who pay tribute to the Titans.

t_ch: 哦，这个也算是奥赫玛独特的景致了。
t_en: Oh, this is another unique worldview native to Okhema.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的云顶之城——它的选民总是充满信仰的热情。大部分吧。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s cloudtop city — that god's chosen people are always filled with religious fervor. Most of them.

t_ch: 有时候真羡慕他们能如此投入自己的信仰…至少让生活不那么虚无，对吧？
t_en: Sometimes I really envy how they can throw themselves into their faith like that... At least, it must make life feel a little bit less empty, right?

t_ch: 只可惜信仰就像一种拼图——一旦被搅乱、打碎，人们就很难原模原样地再把它拼装回去。
t_en: It's just a shame that faith is like a jigsaw puzzle — once it's disturbed or smashed, it's very difficult for people to piece it all back together.

t_ch: …虔诚的烟火气，它独特的美丽就来源于此。古代有些贤者会说什么「无知是福」——大概也就是这种意思吧？
t_en: ...That's the essence of devout warmth and its unique charm. Some sages of old would say "Ignorance is bliss"... is that what they meant?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 不打扰你看风景了。
t_en: I won't disturb you from enjoying the view.

t_ch: …坐在屋顶上的人看风景，而看风景的人在街巷中看你。
t_en: ...People sitting on the rooftop look at the view, while people looking at the view see you in the streets.

t_ch: 没什么，我也不打扰你。拜拜。
t_en: It's nothing, I won't bother you. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 咦，是个生面孔。你好啊，我叫卢克雷提娅，是这里的兽医。
t_en: Oh, a fresh face? Hello there, my name's Lucretia and I'm the vet around here.

t_ch: 你是第一次来吗？特意来参观我们工坊的宝贝的吗——就是那些大地兽，它们可是我的大宝贝。毕竟…大地兽是吉奥里亚赐予我们的礼物嘛！
t_en: Is this your first time here? Did you come to see the precious babies in our workshop, our dromases? They're my big babies. I mean... Dromases are a gift from Georios!

t_ch: 是的，我刚来奥赫玛不久。
t_en: Yeah, I just came to Okhema not long ago.

t_ch: 原来如此，我就说你穿着打扮不像本地人。
t_en: I see. I was wondering why you aren't dressed up like a local.

t_ch: 不，我只是刚好路过。
t_en: No, I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 没关系，你要是感兴趣的话可以在这儿多逛逛，「小家伙」们也喜欢热闹。
t_en: That's fine. If you want, you can just hang around here for a while. The little fellas like a bit of action.

t_ch: 不管怎么样，能认识新朋友总归是件好事。来，我给你介绍一下——旁边这些温驯的大家伙们可是我们工坊的至宝，别看它们块头大，平时可乖了。
t_en: In any case, it's always nice to make a new friend. Here, let me introduce you to these docile big 'uns next to you. They're real sweeties around the workshop and don't let their big size fool you, because they're normally very obedient.

t_ch: 好呀，我可以摸摸它们吗？
t_en: Okay. Can I pet them?

t_ch: 抱歉…最近恐怕不行，小家伙们最近表现有些异常，我怕出什么岔子。
t_en: I'm sorry... Not right now. The little fellas have been acting out a little recently, and I'm afraid it could cause trouble.

t_ch: 抱歉，我其实只是路过……
t_en: I'm sorry. I'm actually just passing through...

t_ch: 这样啊，抱歉，我还以为…唔，希望没打扰到你！
t_en: I see, sorry. I thought that... Well, I hope I didn't bug you there!

t_ch: 前些日子，我在给它们做检查的时候，表现得就很奇怪…放在以前，它们虽然害怕，但还是乖乖地排队，顶多就小声哼唧两句…不像现在……
t_en: A few days back when I was checking in on them, they were acting out a little... In the past, whenever they were afraid, they'd still line up obediently and would just grumble a few words... But now...

t_ch: 前些日子，我给它们做检查，小家伙们表现就很奇怪。放在以前，它们虽然害怕，但还是乖乖地排队，顶多就小声哼唧两句，不像现在这么狂躁。
t_en: A few days back when I was checking in on them, the little fellas were acting out a little... In the past, whenever they were afraid, they'd still line up obediently and would just grumble a few notes... But now they've turned feral...

t_ch: 为了你的安全起见，现在还是眼看手勿动吧。
t_en: Out of consideration for your safety, please look but don't touch.

t_ch: 它们出什么事了？
t_en: Did something happen to them?

t_ch: 「表现怪怪的」是指什么？
t_en: What do you mean by "acting out"?

t_ch: 就是…突然出现了一些我从未见过的行为。要知道，我当兽医的年数虽然不算太长，但我家世代专门诊治大地兽，不客气地说，我见过的大地兽恐怕比我认识的朋友还多……
t_en: Well... They suddenly started behaving in a way they've never done before. You know, I may not have been a vet for very long, but my family has been looking after dromases for generations. To be blunt, I've probably got more of them as friends than I do people...

t_ch: 可前不久有个商队跟我反映的——他们商队的大地兽在回奥赫玛的路上，突然停下不走了，一直在「唱歌」，怎么哄都哄不动。
t_en: But not long ago, a caravan reported back to me that on the way back to Okhema, their dromases stopped and refused to walk. They started "singing" instead. They couldn't be pressed on no matter what.

t_ch: 那声音…据他们所说，非常尖锐，但你要知道它们平时的声音都是很低沉的，不会发出这么高音域的叫声。
t_en: That sound... according to the caravan, was really sharp. But you have to understand that normally their voices are very low, and they don't usually make high-pitched shrieks.

t_ch: 他们这个说辞我起初还不信，可昨天约莫是幕匿时分，我就听到了它们在这么叫，吓了我一跳。
t_en: At first, I didn't believe what they said. But yesterday around the Curtain-Fall Hour, I heard them doing it, and it freaked me out.

t_ch: 那你有查出来原因吗？
t_en: Have you been able to find out why?

t_ch: 还没呢，我正在记录它们的异常行为，希望能从中总结出某些规律。
t_en: Not yet. I've been recording their anomalous behaviors, and hope to be able to put together some conclusions then.

t_ch: 听着是有些奇怪……
t_en: It sounds a little weird...

t_ch: 对吧！它们平时都很乖的，大家都夸它们听话，谁知道会引吭高歌突发扰民——附近的居民跟我们抱怨了好几次呢。
t_en: Right! They're usually so well-behaved and praised for their obedience. I never saw this coming, with the loud singing disturbing people — we've had to field complaints from neighbors several times now.

t_ch: 目前我有点怀疑，这是不是某种古老的习性——也许古老到在吉奥里亚将它们赐予我们之前，它们就生性如此，只是被驯养了这么多年，因为生活原因，很少对人展示出这一点。
t_en: Right now, I'm a little skeptical about whether it's some kind of ancient habit — perhaps a habit so ancient that it was how they naturally were prior to Georios gifting them to us. But, through years of domestication, they have rarely displayed this to humans, likely due to changes in their living conditions.

t_ch: 因为那声音…我听着感觉就像是远古的号角在呼唤着什么一样，我能感觉到它们有某种情绪包含在内，难道是…今年又要来一次「躁动的创生季」？
t_en: Because that noise... It sounds just like an ancient horn, rousing me to action. I can feel some special kind of emotion within it. Don't tell me... this year we'll be getting yet another "restless Creation Season"?

t_ch: 又到了动物交配的季节…
t_en: Time for another mating season...

t_ch: 「躁动的创生季」，有趣…
t_en: "Restless Creation Season"? Interesting...
